per token/head.\n180:     - BY_HEAD=False: grid is (M,), one program per token looping over heads.\n181: \n182:     Layout assumption: each tensor's last dim packs (num_heads, head_dim)\n183:     contiguously so per-head offset is `h * HEAD_DIM`. The token (M) stride is\n184:     taken from stride_*_m so the kernel works on strided views (e.g. slices of a\n185:     larger qkv buffer produced by `qkv.split`) without requiring `.contiguous()`\n186:     copies. V uses `weight=ones` semantics so the multiply-by-weight is omitted.\n187:     \"\"\"\n188:     m = tl.program_id(0)\n189:     cols = tl.arange(0, BLOCK)\n190:     mask = cols < HEAD_DIM\n191: \n192:     if BY_HEAD:\n193:         h_all = tl.program_id(1)\n194:         if h_all < NUM_Q_HEADS:\n195:             _gemma_qkv_rmsnorm_store(\n196:                 Q_ptr, Q_w_ptr, stride_q_m, m, h_all, cols, mask, HEAD_DIM, eps, True\n197:             )\n198:         elif HAS_KV and h_all < NUM_Q_HEADS + NUM_KV_HEADS:\n199:             h = h_all - NUM_Q_HEADS\n200:             _gemma_qkv_rmsnorm_store(\n201:                 K_ptr, K_w_ptr, stride_k_m, m, h, cols, mask, HEAD_DIM, eps, True\n202:             )\n203:         elif HAS_KV:\n204:             h = h_all -         )\n213: \n214:         if HAS_KV:\n215:             for h in tl.static_range(NUM_KV_HEADS):\n216:                 _gemma_qkv_rmsnorm_store(\n217:                     K_ptr,\n218: _gemma_qkv_rmsnorm_store(\n231:                     V_ptr,\n232:                     Q_w_ptr,\n233:                     stride_v_m,\n234:                     m,\n235:                     h,\n236:                     cols,\n237:                     mask,\n238:                     HEAD_DIM,\n239:                     eps,\n240:                     False,\n241:                 )\n242: \n243: \n244: def gemma_qkv_rmsnorm(\n245:     q: torch.Tensor,\n246:     k: Optional[torch.Tensor],\n247:     v: Optional[torch.Tensor],\n248:     q_weight: torch.Tensor,\n249:     k_weight: Optional[torch.Tensor],\n250:     num_q_heads: int,\n251:     num_kv_heads: int,\n252:     head_dim: int,\n253:     eps: float = 1e-6,\n254: ) -> None:\n255:     \"\"\"In-place fused RMSNorm on Q, K, V for Gemma4 attention.\n256: \n257:     All three norms compute `x * rsqrt(mean(x^2) + eps)` independently per head.\n258:     Q is scaled by `q_weight`, K by `k_weight`, V by 1 (Gemma4's V-norm has\n259:     `with_scale=False`).\n260: \n261:     Inputs may be 2D `(M, num_heads * head_dim)` or strided views of a larger\n262:     buffer (such as q/k/v slices from `qkv.split`). The kernel uses the actual\n263:     `stride(0)` so no `.contiguous()` copy is required. Within a token, the\n264:     last dim must be contiguous so heads pack as `h * head_dim` offsets.\n265: \n266:     If k and v are both None (KV-shared layer), only Q is normalized.\n267:     \"\"\"\n268:     assert q.is_cuda or q.is_xpu\n269:     assert q.stride(-1) == 1, \"Q's last dim must be contiguous\"\n270:     assert q_weight.shape[-1] == head_dim\n271:     M = q.shape[0] if q.dim() >= 2 else 1\n272:     BLOCK = triton.next_power_of_2(head_dim)\n273: \n274:     has_kv = k is not None and v is not None\n275:     if has_kv:\n276:         assert (k.is_cuda and v.is_cuda) or (k.is_xpu and v.is_xpu)\n277:         assert k.stride(-1) == 1 and v.stride(-1) == 1\n278:         assert k_weight is not None and k_weight.shape[-1] == head_dim\n279: \n280:     if M <= 256:\n281:         total_heads = num_q_heads + (2 * num_kv_heads if has_kv else 0)\n282:         _gemma_qkv_rmsnorm_kernel[(M, total_heads)](\n283:             q,\n284:             k if has_kv else q,\n285:             v if has_kv else q,\n286:             q_weight,\n287:             k_weight if has_kv else q_weight,\n288:             q.stride(0),\n289:             k.stride(0) if has_kv else 0,\n290:             v.stride(0) if has_kv else 0,\n291:             NUM_Q_HEADS=num_q_heads,\n292:             NUM_KV_HEADS=num_kv_heads if has_kv else 0,\n293:             HEAD_DIM=head_dim,\n294:             eps=eps,\n295:             HAS_KV=has_kv,\n296:             BY_HEAD=True,\n297:             BLOCK=BLOCK,\n298:         )\n299:         return\n300: \n301:     _gemma_qkv_rmsnorm_kernel[(M,)](\n302:         q,\n303:         k if has_kv else q,\n304:         v if has_kv else q,\n305:         q_weight,\n306:         k_weight if has_kv else q_weight,\n307:         q.stride(0),\n308:         k.stride(0) if has_kv else 0,\n309:         v.stride(0) if has_kv else 0,\n310:         NUM_Q_HEADS=num_q_heads,\n311:         NUM_KV_HEADS=num_kv_heads if has_kv else 0,\n312:         HEAD_DIM=head_dim,\n313:         eps=eps,\n314:         HAS_KV=has_kv,\n315:         BY_HEAD=False,\n316:         BLOCK=BLOCK,\n317:     )\n318: \n319: \n320: @triton.jit\n321: def _gemma_routing_post_topk_kernel(\n322:     Logits_ptr,\n323:     Ids_ptr,\n324:     Scale_ptr,\n325:     Out_weights_ptr,\n326:     Out_ids_ptr,\n327:     stride_l,\n328:     stride_ow,\n329:     stride_oi,\n330:     K: tl.constexpr,\n331:     BLOCK_K: tl.constexpr,\n332: ):\n333:     \"\"\"Fused: softmax(topk_logits) * per_expert_scale[topk_ids] → float32 weights, int32 ids.\n334: \n335:     One program per token. K is the number of top-k experts (e.g. 8).\n336:     \"\"\"\n337:     row = tl.program_id(0)\n338:     cols = tl.arange(0, BLOCK_K)\n339:     mask = cols < K\n340: \n341:     logits = tl.load(\n342:         Logits_ptr + row * stride_l + cols, mask=mask, other=float(\"-inf\")\n343:     ).to(tl.float32)\n344:     ids_i64 = tl.load(Ids_ptr + row * stride_l + cols, mask=mask, other=0)\n345: \n346:     # Stable softmax\n347:     max_val = tl.max(logits, axis=0)\n348:     exp_val = tl.exp(logits - max_val)\n349:     sum_exp = tl.sum(exp_val, axis=0)\n350:     weights = exp_val / sum_exp\n351: \n352:     # Gather per_expert_scale and multiply\n353:     scale = tl.load(Scale_ptr + ids_i64, mask=mask, other=1.0).to(tl.float32)\n354:     weights = weights * scale\n355: \n356:     tl.store(Out_weights_ptr + row * stride_ow + cols, weights, mask=mask)\n357:     tl.store(Out_ids_ptr + row * stride_oi + cols, ids_i64.to(tl.int32), mask=mask)\n358: \n359: \n360: def gemma_routing_post_topk(\n361:     topk_logits: torch.Tensor,\n362:     topk_ids: torch.Tensor,\n363:     per_expert_scale: torch.Tensor,\n364: ) -> tuple[torch.Tensor, torch.Tensor]:\n365:     \"\"\"Fused softmax + scale-gather + casts for Gemma4 routing.\n366: \n367:     Replaces: softmax(topk_logits) * per_expert_scale[topk_ids] → (f32, i32).\n368:     \"\"\"\n369:     B, K = topk_logits.shape\n370:     BLOCK_K = triton.next_power_of_2(K)\n371:     out_weights = torch.empty((B, K), dtype=torch.float32, device=topk_logits.device)\n372:     out_ids = torch.empty((B, K), dtype=torch.int32, device=topk_logits.device)\n373: \n374:     _gemma_routing_post_topk_kernel[(B,)](\n375:         topk_logits,\n376:         topk_ids,\n377:         per_expert_scale,\n378:   )\n386:     return out_weights, out_ids\n387: \n388: \n389: def gemma_dual_rmsnorm_residual_scalar(\n390:     x1: torch.Tensor,\n391:     weight1: torch.Tensor,\n392:     x2: torch.Tensor,\n393:        residual.stride(0),\n419:         out.stride(0),\n420:         N,\n421:         eps1,\n422:         eps2,\n423:         eps3,\n424:         BLOCK_SIZE=BLOCK_SIZE,\n425:     )\n426:     return out\n427: \n428: \n429: @triton.jit\n430: def _gemma4_routing_kernel(\n431:     gating_ptr,  # [T, E] router logits, any float dtype\n432:     per_expert_scale_ptr,  # [E] per-expert scale (any float dtype)\n433:     topk_weights_ptr,  # [T, K] fp32 out\n434:     topk_ids_ptr,  # [T, K] int32 out\n435:     stride_g_t,  # stride of gating in the token dim\n436:     E: tl.constexpr,\n437:     K: tl.constexpr,\n438:     BLOCK_E: tl.constexpr,\n439: ):\n440:     pid = tl.program_id(0)\n441:     offs_e = tl.arange(0, BLOCK_E)\n442:     valid = offs_e < E\n443: \n444:     logits = tl.load(\n445:         gating_ptr + pid * stride_g_t + offs_e,\n446:         mask=valid,\n447:         other=-float(\"inf\"),\n448:     ).to(tl.float32)\n449: \n450:     # Pack (sort_key, expert_id) into one int64 so a single signed-ascending\n451:     # tl.sort yields logits in descending float order. The key bijection is\n452:     # anti-monotone on the float value, and the <<32 shift moves its high bit\n453:     # into the int64 sign bit. Ties break by expert id ascending. Invalid\n454:     # lanes use a max key so they sort last.\n455:     MIN32 = -2147483648\n456:     logit_bits = logits.to(tl.int32, bitcast=True)\n457:     sign = logit_bits >> 31\n458:     key = tl.where(sign == 0, logit_bits ^ -1, logit_bits ^ MIN32)\n459:     key = tl.where(valid, key, 0x7FFFFFFF)\n460:     sk64 = key.to(tl.int64) & 0x00000000FFFFFFFF\n461:     packed = (sk64 << 32) | offs_e.to(tl.int64)\n462: \n463:     sorted_p = tl.sort(packed, descending=False)\n464:     all_keys = ((sorted_p >> 32) & 0x00000000FFFFFFFF).to(tl.int32)\n465:     all_ids = (sorted_p & 0x00000000FFFFFFFF).to(tl.int32)\n466: \n467:     # Invert the key bijection to recover the original logit value.\n468:     sign_k = all_keys >> 31\n469:     all_bits = tl.where(sign_k < 0, all_keys ^ -1, all_keys ^ MIN32)\n470:     all_logits = all_bits.to(tl.float32, bitcast=True)\n471: \n472:     # softmax over the top-K logits; max sits at index 0 (sorted descending).\n473:     top_mask = offs_e < K\n474:     max_l = tl.max(tl.where(top_mask, all_logits, -float(\"inf\")), axis=0)\n475:     raw_exp = tl.where(top_mask, tl.exp(all_logits - max_l), 0.0)\n476: \n477:     denom = tl.sum(raw_exp, axis=0)\n478:     denom = tl.where(denom > 0.0, denom, 1.0)\n479:     weights = raw_exp / denom\n480: \n481:     scales = tl.load(\n482:         per_expert_scale_ptr + all_ids.to(tl.int64),\n483:         mask=top_mask,\n484:         other=1.0,\n485:     ).to(tl.float32)\n486:     weights = weights * scales\n487: \n488:     base_off = pid * K + offs_e\n489:     tl.store(topk_weights_ptr + base_off, weights, mask=top_mask)\n490:     tl.store(topk_ids_ptr + base_off, all_ids, mask=top_mask)\n491: \n492: \n493: def gemma4_fused_routing(\n494:     gating_output: torch.Tensor,\n495:     per_expert_scale: torch.Tensor,\n496:     topk: int,\n497: ) -> tuple[torch.Tensor, torch.Tensor]:\n498:     \"\"\"One-launch Gemma4 router.\n499: \n500:     Args:\n501:      topk: number of experts to keep per token.\n505: \n506:     Returns:\n507:         topk_weights: [T, topk] fp32 (matches SGLang TopK contract).\n508:         topk_ids: [T, topk] int32 (matches SGLang TopK contract).\n509:     \"\"\"\n510:     assert gating_output.dim() == 2, \"expected [T, E] router logits\"\n511:     assert per_expert_scale.dim() == 1\n512:     assert per_expert_scale.shape[0] == gating_output.shape[1]\n513:     T, E = gating_output.shape\n514:     assert topk <= E, f\"topk ({topk}) must be <= E ({E})\"\n515:     assert E <= 1024, f\"gemma4_fused_routing only supports E<=1024, got E={E}\"\n516: \n517:     gating_output = gating_output.contiguous()\n518:     per_expert_scale = per_expert_scale.contiguous()\n519: \n520:     BLOCK_E = triton.next_power_of_2(E)\n521:     topk_weights = torch.empty(\n522:         (T, topk), dtype=torch.float32, device=gating_output.device\n523:     )\n524:     topk_ids = torch.empty((T, topk), dtype=torch.int32, device=gating_output.device)\n525: \n526:     if T == 0:\n527:         return topk_weights, topk_ids\n528: \n529:     _gemma4_routing_kernel[(T,)](\n530:         gating_output,\n531:         per_expert_scale,\n532:         topk_weights,\n533:         topk_ids,\n534:         gating_output.stride(0),\n535:         E,\n536:         topk,\n537:         BLOCK_E,\n538:         num_warps=1,\n539:     )\n540:     return topk_weights, topk_ids\n\n(End of file - total 540 lines)\n</content>","metadata":{"preview":"\"\"\"Fused triton kernels for Gemma4 torch\nimport triton\nimport triton.language as tl\n\n\n@triton.jit\ndef _gemma_rmsnorm_residual_kernel(\n    X_ptr,\n    W_ptr,\n    Residual_ptr,\n    Scalar_ptr,\n    Out_ptr,\n    stride_x,\n    stride_r,\n    stride_o,\n    N,\n    eps,\n    HAS_SCALAR: tl.constexpr,\n    BLOCK_SIZE: tl.constexpr,\n):\n    \"\"\"Fused kernel: out = rmsnorm(x, w) + residual [* scalar]\n\n    When HAS_SCALAR is True, also multiplies by a scalar loaded from Scalar_ptr.\n    \"\"\"\n    row = tl.program_id(0)\n    cols = tl.arange(0, BLOCK_SIZE)\n    mask = cols < N\n\n    x = tl.load(X_ptr + row * stride_x + cols, mask=mask, other=0.0).to(tl.float32)\n    w = tl.load(W_ptr + cols, mask=mask, other=0.0).to(tl.float32)\n    r = tl.load(Residual_ptr + row * stride_r + cols, mask=mask, other=0.0).to(\n        tl.float32\n    )\n\n    var = tl.sum(x * x, axis=0) / N\n    rrms = tl.rsqrt(var + eps)\n    out = x * rrms * w + r\n\n    if HAS_SCALAR:\n        scalar = tl.load(Scalar_ptr).to(tl.float32)\n        out = out * scalar\n\n    tl.store(Out_ptr + row * stride_o + cols, out.to(x.dtype), mask=mask)\n\n\ndef gemma_rmsnorm_residual_scalar(\n    x: torch.Tensor,\n    weight: torch.Tensor,\n BLOCK_SIZE=BLOCK_SIZE,\n    )\n    return out\n\n\n@triton.jit\ndef _gemma_dual_rmsnorm_residual_kernel(\n    X1_ptr,\n    W1_ptr,\n    X2_ptr,\n    W2_ptr,\n    W3_ptr,\n    Residual_ptr,\n    Scalar_ptr,\n    Out_ptr,\n    stride_x1,\n    stride_x2,\n    stride_r,\n    stride_o,\n    N,\n    eps1,\n    eps2,\n    eps3,\n    BLOCK_SIZE: tl.constexpr,\n):\n    \"\"\"Fused: out = (rmsnorm(rmsnorm(x1,w1) + rmsnorm(x2,w2), w3) + residual) * scalar\"\"\"\n    row = tl.program_id(0)\n    cols = tl.arange(0, BLOCK_SIZE)\n    mask = cols < N\n\n    x1 = tl.load(X1_ptr + row * stride_x1 + cols, mask=mask, other=0.0).to(tl.float32)\n    w1 = tl.load(W1_ptr + cols, mask=mask, other=0.0).to(tl.float32)\n    x2 = tl.load(X2_ptr + row * stride_x2 + cols, mask=mask, row * stride_r + cols, mask=mask, other=0.0).to(\n        tl.float32\n    )\n\n    var1 = tl.sum(x1 * x1, axis=0) / N\n    norm1 = x1 * tl.rsqrt(var1 + eps1) * w1\n\n    var2 = tl.sum(x2 * x2, axis=0) / N\n    norm2 = x2 * tl.rsqrt(var2 + eps2) * w2\n\n    combined = norm1 + norm2\n\n    var3 = tl.sum(combined * combined, axis=0) / N\n    norm3 = combined * tl.rsqrt(var3 + eps3) * w3\n\n    scalar = tl.load(Scalar_ptr).to(tl.float32)\n    out = (norm3 + r) * scalar\n\n    tl.store(Out_ptr + row * stride_o + cols, out.to(x1.dtype), mask=mask)\n\n\n@triton.jit\ndef _gemma_qkv_rmsnorm_store(\n    X_ptr,\n    W_ptr,\n    stride_m,\n    m,\n    h,\n    cols,\n    mask,\n    HEAD_DIM: tl.constexpr,\n    eps,\n    HAS_WEIGHT: tl.constexpr,\n):\n    off = m * stride_m + h * HEAD_DIM + cols\n    x = tl.load(X_ptr + off, mask=mask, other=0.0).to(tl.float32)\n    rrms = tl.rsqrt(tl.sum(x * x, axis=0) / HEAD_DIM + eps)\n    out = x * rrms\n    if HAS_WEIGHT:\n        w = tl.load(W_ptr + cols, K_ptr,\n    V_ptr,\n    Q_w_ptr,\n    K_w_ptr,\n    stride_q_m,\n    stride_k_m,\n    stride_v_m,\n    NUM_Q_HEADS: tl.constexpr,\n    NUM_KV_HEADS: tl.constexpr,\n    HEAD_DIM: tl.constexpr,\n    eps,\n    HAS_KV: tl.constexpr,\n    BY_HEAD: tl.constexpr,\n    BLOCK: tl.constexpr,\n):\n    \"\"\"Fused per-head RMSNorm for Q, K, V.\n\n    The same kernel supports two launch shapes:\n    - BY_HEAD=True: grid is (M, total_heads), one program per token/head.\n    - BY_HEAD=False: grid is (M,), one program per token looping over heads.\n\n    Layout assumption: each tensor's last dim packs (num_heads, head_dim)\n    contiguously so per-head offset is `h * HEAD_DIM`. The token (M) stride is\n    taken from stride_*_m so the kernel works on strided views (e.g. slices of a\n    larger qkv buffer produced by `qkv.split`) without requiring `.contiguous()`\n    copies. V uses `weight=ones` semantics so the multiply-by-weight is omitted.\n    \"\"\"\n    m = tl.program_id(0)\n    cols = tl.arange(0, BLOCK)\n    mask = cols < HEAD_DIM\n\n    if BY_HEAD:\n        h_all = tl.program_id(1)\n        if h_all < NUM_Q_HEADS:\n            _gemma_qkv_rmsnorm_store(\n                Q_ptr, Q_w_ptr, stride_q_m, m, h_all, cols, mask, HEAD_DIM, eps, True\n            )\n        elif HAS_KV and h_all < NUM_Q_HEADS + NUM_KV_HEADS:\n            h = h_all - NUM_Q_HEADS\n            _gemma_qkv_rmsnorm_store(\n                K_ptr, K_w_ptr, stride_k_m, m, h, cols, mask, HEAD_DIM, eps, True\n            )\n        elif HAS_KV:\n            h = h_all - NUM_Q_HEADS - NUM_KV_HEADS\n            _gemma_qkv_rmsnorm_store(\n                V_ptr, Q_w_ptr, stride_v_m, m, h, cols, mask, HEAD_DIM, eps, False\n            )\n    else:\n        for h in tl.static_range(NUM_Q_HEADS):\n            _gemma_qkv_rmsnorm_store(\n                Q_ptr, Q_w_ptr, stride_q_m, m, h, cols, mask, HEAD_DIM, eps, True\n            )\n\n        if HAS_KV:\n            for h in tl.static_range(NUM_KV_HEADS):\n                _gemma_qkv_rmsnorm_store(\n                    K_ptr,\n                    K_w_ptr,\n                    stride_k_m,\n                    m,\n                    h,\n                    cols,\n                    mask,\n                    HEAD_DIM,\n                    eps,\n                    True,\n                )\n\n            for h in tl.static_range(NUM_KV_HEADS):\n                _gemma_qkv_rmsnorm_store(\n                    V_ptr,\n                    Q_w_ptr,\n                    stride_v_m,\n                    m,\n                    h,\n                    cols,\n                    mask,\n                    HEAD_DIM,\n                    eps,\n                    False,\n                )\n\n\ndef gemma_qkv_rmsnorm(\n    q: torch.Tensor,\n    k: Optional[torch.Tensor],\n    v: Optional[torch.Tensor],\n    q_weight: torch.Tensor,\n    k_weight: Optional[torch.Tensor],\n    num_q_heads: int,\n    num_kv_heads: int,\n    head_dim: int,\n    eps: float = 1e-6,\n) -> None:\n    \"\"\"In-place fused RMSNorm on Q, K, V for Gemma4 attention.\n\n    All three norms compute `x * rsqrt(mean(x^2) + eps)` independently per head.\n    Q is scaled by `q_weight`, K by `k_weight`, V by 1 (Gemma4's V-norm has\n    `with_scale=False`).\n\n    Inputs may be 2D `(M, num_heads * head_dim)` or strided views of a larger\n    buffer (such as q/k/v slices from `qkv.split`). The kernel uses the actual\n    `stride(0)` so no `.contiguous()` copy is required. Within a token, the\n    last dim must be contiguous so heads pack as `h * head_dim` offsets.\n\n    If k and v are both None (KV-shared layer), only Q is normalized.\n    \"\"\"\n    assert q.is_cuda or q.is_xpu\n    assert q.stride(-1) == 1, \"Q's last dim must be contiguous\"\n    assert q_weight.shape[-1] == head_dim\n    M = q.shape[0] if q.dim() >= 2 else 1\n    BLOCK = triton.next_power_of_2(head_dim)\n\n    has_kv = k is not None and v is not None\n    if has_kv:\n        assert (k.is_cuda and v.is_cuda) or (k.is_xpu and v.is_xpu)\n        assert k.stride(-1) == 1 and v.stride(-1) == 1\n        assert k_weight is not None and k_weight.shape[-1] == head_dim\n\n    if M <= 256:\n        total_heads = num_q_heads + (2 * num_kv_heads if has_kv else 0)\n        _gemma_qkv_rmsnorm_kernel[(M, total_heads)](\n            q,\n            k if has_kv else q,\n            v if has_kv else q,\n            q_weight,\n            k_weight if has_kv else q_weight,\n            q.stride(0),\n            k.stride(0) if has_kv else 0,\n            v.stride(0) if has_kv else 0,\n            NUM_Q_HEADS=num_q_heads,\n            NUM_KV_HEADS=num_kv_heads if has_kv else 0,\n            HEAD_DIM=head_dim,\n            eps=eps,\n            HAS_KV=has_kv,\n            BY_HEAD=True,\n            BLOCK=BLOCK,\n        )\n        return\n\n    _gemma_qkv_rmsnorm_kernel[(M,)](\n        q,\n        k if has_kv else q,\n        v if has_kv else q,\n        q_weight,\n        k_weight if has_kv else q_weight,\n        q.stride(0),\n        k.stride(0) if has_kv else 0,\n        v.stride(0) if has_kv else 0,\n        NUM_Q_HEADS=num_q_heads,\n        NUM_KV_HEADS=num_kv_heads if has_kv else 0,\n        HEAD_DIM=head_dim,\n        eps=eps,\n        HAS_KV=has_kv,\n        BY_HEAD=False,\n        BLOCK=BLOCK,\n    )\n\n\n@triton.jit\ndef _gemma_routing_post_topk_kernel(\n    Logits_ptr,\n    Ids_ptr,\n    Scale_ptr,\n    Out_weights_ptr,\n    Out_ids_ptr,\n    stride_l,\n    stride_ow,\n    stride_oi,\n    K: tl.constexpr,\n    BLOCK_K: tl.constexpr,\n):\n    \"\"\"Fused: softmax(topk_logits) * per_expert_scale[topk_ids] → float32 weights, int32 ids.\n\n    One program per token. K is the number of top-k experts (e.g. 8).\n    \"\"\"\n    row = tl.program_id(0)\n    cols = tl.arange(0, BLOCK_K)\n    mask = cols < K\n\n    logits = tl.load(\n        Logits_ptr + row * stride_l + cols, mask=mask, other=float(\"-inf\")\n    ).to(tl.float32)\n    ids_i64 = tl.load(Ids_ptr + row * stride_l + cols, mask=mask, other=0)\n\n    # Stable softmax\n    max_val = tl.max(logits, axis=0)\n    exp_val = tl.exp(logits - max_val)\n    sum_exp = tl.sum(exp_val, axis=0)\n    weights = exp_val / sum_exp\n\n    # Gather per_expert_scale and multiply\n    scale = tl.load(Scale_ptr + ids_i64, mask=mask, other=1.0).to(tl.float32)\n    weights = weights * scale\n\n    tl.store(Out_weights_ptr + row * stride_ow + cols, weights, mask=mask)\n    tl.store(Out_ids_ptr + row * stride_oi + cols, ids_i64.to(tl.int32), mask=mask)\n\n\ndef gemma_routing_post_topk(\n    topk_logits: torch.Tensor,\n    topk_ids: torch.Tensor,\n    per_expert_scale: torch.Tensor,\n) -> tuple[torch.Tensor, torch.Tensor]:\n    \"\"\"Fused softmax + scale-gather + casts for Gemma4 routing.\n\n    Replaces: softmax(topk_logits) * per_expert_scale[topk_ids] → (f32, i32).\n    \"\"\"\n    B, K = topk_logits.stride(0),\n        out_weights.stride(0),\n        out_ids.stride(0),\n        K=K,\n        BLOCK_K=BLOCK_K,\n    )\n    return out_weights, out_ids\n\n\ndef gemma_dual_rmsnorm_residual_scalar(\n    x1: torch.Tensor,\n    weight1: torch.Tensor,\n    x2: torch.Tensor,\n    weight2: torch.Tensor,\n    weight3: torch.Tensor,\n    residual: torch.Tensor,\n    scalar: torch.Tensor,\n    eps1: float = 1e-6,\n    eps2: float = 1e-6,\n    eps3: float = 1e-6,\n) -> torch.Tensor:\n    \"\"\"Fused (rmsnorm(rmsnorm(x1,w1) + rmsnorm(x2,w2), w3) + residual) * scalar.\"\"\"\n    assert x1.dim() == 2 and x1.stride(-1) == 1\n    M, N = x1.shape\n    BLOCK_SIZE = triton.next_power_of_2(N)\n    out = torch.empty_like(x1)\n\n    _gemma_dual_rmsnorm_residual_kernel[(M,)](\n        x1,\n        weight1,\n        x2,\n        weight2,\n        weight3,\n        residual,\n        scalar,\n        out,\n        x1.stride(0),\n        x2.stride(0),\n        residual.stride(0),\n        out.stride(0),\n        N,\n        eps1,\n        eps2,\n        eps3,\n        BLOCK_SIZE=BLOCK_SIZE,\n    )\n    return out\n\n\n@triton.jit\ndef _gemma4_routing_kernel(\n    gating_ptr,  # [T, E] router logits, any float dtype\n    per_expert_scale_ptr,  # [E] per-expert scale (any float dtype)\n    topk_weights_ptr,  # [T, K] fp32 out\n    topk_ids_ptr,  # [T, K] int32 out\n    stride_g_t,  # stride of gating in the token dim\n    E: tl.constexpr,\n    K: tl.constexpr,\n    BLOCK_E: tl.constexpr,\n):\n    pid = tl.program_id(0)\n    offs_e = tl.arange(0, BLOCK_E)\n    valid = offs_e < E\n\n    logits = tl.load(\n        gating_ptr + pid * stride_g_t + offs_e,\n        mask=valid,\n        other=-float(\"inf\"),\n    ).to(tl.float32)\n\n    # Pack (sort_key, expert_id) into one int64 so a single signed-ascending\n    # tl.sort yields logits in descending float order. The key bijection is\n    # anti-monotone on the float value, and the <<32 shift moves its high bit\n    # into the int64 sign bit. Ties break by expert id ascending. Invalid\n    # lanes use a max key so they sort last.\n    MIN32 = -2147483648\n    logit_bits = logits.to(tl.int32, bitcast=True)\n    sign = logit_bits >> 31\n    key = tl.where(sign == 0, logit_bits ^ -1, logit_bits ^ MIN32)\n    key = tl.where(valid, key, 0x7FFFFFFF)\n    sk64 = key.to(tl.int64) & 0x00000000FFFFFFFF\n    packed = (sk64 << 32) | offs_e.to(tl.int64)\n\n    sorted_p = tl.sort(packed, descending=False)\n    all_keys = ((sorted_p >> 32) & 0x00000000FFFFFFFF).to(tl.int32)\n    all_ids = (sorted_p & 0x00000000FFFFFFFF).to(tl.int32)\n\n    # Invert the key bijection to recover the original logit value.\n    sign_k = all_keys >> 31\n    all_bits = tl.where(sign_k < 0, all_keys ^ -1, all_keys ^ MIN32)\n    all_logits = all_bits.to(tl.float32, bitcast=True)\n\n    # softmax over the top-K logits; max sits at index 0 (sorted descending).\n    top_mask = offs_e < K\n    max_l = tl.max(tl.where(top_mask, all_logits, -float(\"inf\")), axis=0)\n    raw_exp = tl.where(top_mask, tl.exp(all_logits - max_l), 0.0)\n\n    denom = tl.sum(raw_exp, axis=0)\n    denom = tl.where(denom > 0.0, denom, 1.0)\n    weights = raw_exp / denom\n\n    scales = tl.load(\n        per_expert_scale_ptr + all_ids.to(tl.int64),\n        mask=top_mask,\n        other=1.0,\n    ).to(tl.float32)\n    weights = weights * scales\n\n    base_off = pid * K + offs_e\n    tl.store(topk_weights_ptr + base_off, weights, mask=top_mask)\n    tl.store(topk_ids_ptr + base_off, all_ids, mask=top_mask)\n\n\ndef gemma4_fused_routing(\n    gating_output: torch.Tensor,\n    per_expert_scale: torch.Tensor,\n    topk: int,\n) -> tuple[torch.Tensor, torch.Tensor]:\n    \"\"\"One-launch Gemma4 router.\n\n    Args:\n        gating_output: [T, E] router logits in any floating dtype; will be\n            cast to fp32 inside the kernel.\n        per_expert_scale: [E] per-expert scale, any floating dtype.\n        topk: number of experts to keep per token.\n\n    Returns:\n        topk_weights: [T, topk] fp32 (matches SGLang TopK contract).\n        topk_ids: [T, topk] int32 (matches SGLang TopK contract).\n    \"\"\"\n    assert gating_output.dim() == 2, \"expected [T, E] router logits\"\n    assert per_expert_scale.dim() == 1\n    assert per_expert_scale.shape[0] == gating_output.shape[1]\n    T, E = gating_output.shape\n    assert topk <= E, f\"topk ({topk}) must be <= E ({E})\"\n    assert E <= 1024, f\"gemma4_fused_routing only supports E<=1024, got E={E}\"\n\n    gating_output = gating_output.contiguous()\n    per_expert_scale = per_expert_scale.contiguous()\n\n    BLOCK_E = triton.next_power_of_2(E)\n    topk_weights = torch.empty(\n        (T, topk), dtype=torch.float32, device=gating_output.device\n    )\n    topk_ids = torch.empty((T, topk), dtype=torch.int32, device=gating_output.device)\n\n    if T == 0:\n        return topk_weights, topk_ids\n\n    _gemma4_routing_kernel[(T,)](\n        gating_output,\n        per_expert_scale,\n        topk_weights,\n        topk_ids,\n        gating_output.stride(0),\n        E,\n        topk,\n        BLOCK_E,\n        num_warps=1,\n    )\n    return topk_weights, topk_ids","lineStart":1,"lineEnd":540,"totalLines":540,"truncated":false}},"title":"sgl-workspace/sglang/python/sglang/srt/layers/gemma4_fused_ops.py","time":{"start":1784476744225,"end":1784476744245}},"id":"prt_f7b1a373a001W3ns2hnz3hSmCh","sessionID":"ses_084e653e2ffeX7v9nOpFRiIG7A","messageID":"msg_f7b1a334b001v5RLtl89Qkuhcg"}}

The router projection is\n193:     external (Gemma4Router) — this class only handles expert dispatch.\n194: \n195:     Gemma4 routing: softmax over ALL experts → top-k → renormalize.\n196:     per_expert_scale is folded into routing weights for mathematical\n197:     correctness with MoE's fused kernel.\n198:     \"\"\"\n199: \n200:     def __init__(\n201:         self,\n202:   per_expert_scale = self.per_expert_scale\n220: \n221:         def routing_function(\n222:             hidden_states: torch.Tensor,\n223:             gating_output: torch.Tensor,\n224:             topk: int,\n225:             renormalize: bool,  # always True for Gemma4; softmax identity only holds when renormalizing\n226:         ) -> tuple[torch.Tensor, torch.Tensor]:\n227:             # softmax(all)[topk] / sum(softmax(all)[topk]) = softmax(topk_logits),\n228:             # so we softmax only the top-k logits (fewer kernel launches).\n229:             if (\n230:                 gating_output.is_cuda\n231:                 and gating_output.dim() == 2\n232:                 and gating_output.dtype\n233:                 in (torch.float16, torch.bfloat16, torch.float32)\n234: \n239:             # Fused: softmax + per_expert_scale gather + mul + casts in one kernel\n240:             if topk_logits.is_cuda or topk_logits.is_xpu:\n241:                 return gemma_routing_post_topk(topk_logits, topk_ids, per_expert_scale)\n242: \n243:             topk_weights = torch.nn.functional.softmax(topk_logits, dim=-1)\n244:             topk_weights = topk_weights make_layers\n\nlogger = logging.getLogger(__name__)\n\n\n# Aligned with HF's implementation, using sliding window inclusive with the last token\n# SGLang assumes exclusive\ndef get_attention_sliding_window_size(config):\n    return config.sliding_window - 1\n\n\nGemma4MLP = Gemma3MLP\nGemma4TextScaledWordEmbedding = Gemma3TextScaledWordEmbedding\n\n\ndef pp_filter_load_weight(\n    name,\n    loaded_weight,\n    *,\n    pp_group,\n    start_layer,\n    end_layer,\n    params_dict,\n    loaded_params,\n    tie_word_embeddings,\n    embed_weight_name,\n    first_rank_only_patterns=(),\n    last_rank_only_prefixes=(),\n    head_param_name=\"lm_head.weight\",\n):\n    \"\"\"Shared PP filter for Gemma4 load_weights paths.\n\n    Returns True if the caller should ``continue`` (handled or skipped),\n    False otherwise.  No-op when ``pp_group.world_size == 1``.\n\n    Handles three concerns in order:\n      1. Drop transformer-layer weights outside [start_layer, end_layer).\n      2. Route the tied ``embed_tokens.weight`` to ``lm_head`` on the last\n         rank (under PP, embed and lm_head live on different ranks so they\n         can't be tied via module aliasing).\n      3. Skip rank-local module weights on the wrong rank.\n    \"\"\"\n    if pp_group.world_size <= 1:\n        return False\n\n    layer_id = get_layer_id(name)\n    if layer_id is not None and (layer_id < start_layer or layer_id >= end_layer):\n        return True\n\n    if tie_word_embeddings and pp_group.is_last_rank and name == embed_weight_name:\n        head_param = params_dict.get(head_param_name)\n        if head_param is not None:\n            wl = getattr(head_param, \"weight_loader\", default_weight_loader)\n            wl(head_param, loaded_weight)\n            loaded_params.add(head_param_name)\n        return True\n\n    if not pp_group.is_first_rank and any(p in name for p in first_rank_only_patterns):\n        return True\n\n    if not pp_group.is_last_rank and any(\n        name.startswith(p) for p in last_rank_only_prefixes\n    ):\n        return True\n\n    return False\n\n\nclass Gemma4Router(nn.Module):\n    \"\"\"Router for Gemma4 MoE that preprocesses input before projection.\n\n    Applies RMSNorm (no learned weight), root_size scaling\n    (hidden_size^{-0.5}), then a learned per-dimension scale before\n    projecting to expert logits.\n\n    This preprocessing is applied ONLY to the router's input, not to\n    the expert MLPs' input.\n    \"\"\"\n\n    def __init__(\n        self,\n        config,\n        quant_config: QuantizationConfig | None = None,\n        prefix: str = \"\",\n    ) -> None:\n        super().__init__()\n        self.hidden_size = config.hidden_size\n\n        # RMSNorm without learned weight — scale is folded into norm weight\n        # after loading so forward is a single fused norm kernel.\n        self.norm = Gemma4RMSNorm(\n            self.hidden_size, eps=config.rms_norm_eps, with_scale=False\n        )\n        # Per-dimension learned scale, applied after norm + root_size\n        self.scale = nn.Parameter(torch.ones(self.hidden_size))\n        # Constant 1/sqrt(hidden_size) scaling factor\n        self.register_buffer(\n            \"root_size\",\n            torch.tensor(self.hidden_size**-0.5),\n            persistent=False,\n        )\n        # Project to expert logits; replicated across TP for consistent routing\n        self.proj = ReplicatedLinear(\n            self.hidden_size,\n            config.num_experts,\n            bias=False,\n            quant_config=None,\n            prefix=add_prefix(\"proj\", prefix),\n        )\n        self._scale_fused = False\n\n    def fuse_scale(self):\n        \"\"\"Fold scale * root_size into norm.weight so forward needs no extra mul.\"\"\"\n        fused = (self.scale * self.root_size).to(self.norm.weight.dtype)\n        self.norm.weight.data.copy_(fused)\n        self._scale_fused = True\n\n    def forward(self, x: torch.Tensor) -> torch.Tensor:\n        \"\"\"Returns raw router logits [T, E].\"\"\"\n        if not self._scale_fused:\n            self.fuse_scale()\n        x = self.norm(x)\n        router_logits, _ = self.proj(x)\n        return router_logits\n\n\nclass Gemma4MoE(nn.Module):\n    \"\"\"Mixture of Experts for Gemma4.\n\n    Wraps MoE implementation with custom routing. The router projection is\n    external (Gemma4Router) — this class only handles expert dispatch.\n\n    Gemma4 routing: softmax over ALL experts → top-k → renormalize.\n    per_expert_scale is folded into routing weights for mathematical\n    correctness with MoE's fused kernel.\n    \"\"\"\n\n    def __init__(\n        self,\n        hidden_size: int,\n        layer_id: int,\n        config: Gemma4TextConfig,\n        quant_config: Optional[QuantizationConfig] = None,\n        prefix: str = \"\",\n    ) -> None:\n        super().__init__()\n        self.layer_id = layer_id\n        self.hidden_size = hidden_size\n        self.num_experts = config.num_experts\n        self.tp_size = get_parallel().tp_size\n\n        # Per-expert output scale folded into routing weights so that\n        # MoE's fused kernel computes: Σ_e (expert_e * w_e * scale_e)\n        self.per_expert_scale = nn.Parameter(torch.ones(config.num_experts))\n\n        # Capture param directly to avoid closing over self in the routing closure.\n        per_expert_scale = self.per_expert_scale\n\n        def routing_function(\n            hidden_states: torch.Tensor,\n            gating_output: torch.Tensor,\n            topk: int,\n            renormalize: bool,  # always True for Gemma4; softmax identity only holds when renormalizing\n        ) -> tuple[torch.Tensor, torch.Tensor]:\n            # softmax(all)[topk] / sum(softmax(all)[topk]) = softmax(topk_logits),\n            # so we softmax only the top-k logits (fewer kernel launches).\n            if (\n                gating_output.is_cuda\n                and gating_output.dim() == 2\n                and gating_output.dtype\n                in (torch.float16, torch.bfloat16, torch.float32)\n            ):\n                return gemma4_fused_routing(gating_output, per_expert_scale, topk)\n\n            topk_logits, topk_ids = torch.topk(gating_output, k=topk, dim=-1)\n\n            # Fused: softmax + per_expert_scale gather + mul + casts in one kernel\n            if topk_logits.is_cuda or topk_logits.is_xpu:\n                return gemma_routing_post_topk(topk_logits, topk_ids, per_expert_scale)\n\n            topk_weights = torch.nn.functional.softmax(topk_logits, dim=-1)\n            topk_weights = topk_weights * per_expert_scale[topk_ids].to(\n                topk_weights.dtype\n            )\n            return topk_weights.to(torch.float32), topk_ids.to(torch.int32)\n\n        self.topk = TopK(\n            top_k=config.top_k_experts,\n            layer_id=layer_id,\n            custom_routing_function=routing_function,\n        )\n\n        experts_type = get_moe_impl_class(quant_config)\n\n        self.experts = experts_type(\n            num_experts=config.num_experts\n            + get_global_server_args().ep_num_redundant_experts,\n            hidden_size=config.hidden_size,\n            intermediate_size=config.moe_intermediate_size,\n            layer_id=layer_id,\n            top_k=config.top_k_experts,\n            quant_config=quant_config,\n            prefix=add_prefix(\"experts\", prefix),\n            activation=\"gelu\",\n            reduce_results=True,\n        )\n\n    def forward(\n        self, hidden_states: torch.Tensor, router_logits: torch.Tensor\n    ) -> torch.Tensor:\n        num_tokens, hidden_dim = hidden_states.shape\n        topk_output = self.topk(hidden_states, router_logits)\n        hidden_states = self.experts(hidden_states, topk_output)\n        return hidden_states.view(num_tokens, hidden_dim)\n\n\nclass Gemma4Attention(nn.Module):\n    def __init__(\n        self,\n        layer_id: int,\n        config: Gemma4TextConfig,\n        head_dim: int,\n        max_position_embeddings: int,\n        quant_config: Optional[QuantizationConfig] = None,\n        prefix: str = \"\",\n    ) -> None:\n        super().__init__()\n\n        self.layer_id = layer_id\n        self.config = config\n        tp_size = get_parallel().tp_size\n\n        layer_type = config.layer_types[layer_id]\n        self.sliding_window = (\n            get_attention_sliding_window_size(config)\n            if layer_type == \"sliding_attention\"\n            else -1\n        )\n\n        self.total_num_heads = config.num_attention_heads\n        assert self.total_num_heads % tp_size == 0\n        self.num_heads = self.total_num_heads // tp_size\n\n        if layer_type == \"sliding_attention\":\n            self.total_num_kv_heads = getattr(\n                config, \"swa_num_key_value_heads\", config.num_key_value_heads\n            )\n        else:\n            self.total_num_kv_heads = config.num_key_value_heads\n\n        self.num_kv_heads = max(1, self.total_num_kv_heads // tp_size)\n\n        if self.total_num_kv_heads >= tp_size:\n            assert self.total_num_kv_heads % tp_size == 0\n        else:\n            assert tp_size % self.total_num_kv_heads == 0\n\n        hidden_size = config.hidden_size\n        self.head_dim = head_dim\n\n        self.q_size = self.num_heads * self.head_dim\n        self.kv_size = self.num_kv_heads * self.head_dim\n\n        self.qkv_proj = QKVParallelLinear(\n            hidden_size,\n            self.head_dim,\n            self.total_num_heads,\n            self.total_num_kv_heads,\n            bias=config.attention_bias,\n            quant_config=quant_config,\n            prefix=add_prefix(\"qkv_proj\", prefix),\n        )\n        self.o_proj = RowParallelLinear(\n            self.total_num_heads * self.head_dim,\n            hidden_size,\n            bias=config.attention_bias,\n            quant_config=quant_config,\n            prefix=add_prefix(\"o_proj\", prefix),\n        )\n\n        self.q_norm = Gemma4RMSNorm(\n            self.head_dim,\n            eps=config.rms_norm_eps,\n        )\n        self.k_norm = Gemma4RMSNorm(\n            self.head_dim,\n            eps=config.rms_norm_eps,\n        )\n        self.v_norm = Gemma4RMSNorm(\n            self.head_dim, eps=config.rms_norm_eps, scale_shift=0.0, with_scale=False\n        )\n\n        if layer_type in config.rope_parameters:\n            rope_parameters = dict(config.rope_parameters[layer_type])\n        else:\n            rope_parameters = dict(\n                rope_type=\"default\",\n                rope_theta=10000.0,\n            )\n\n        # KV sharing logic\n        num_kv_shared_layers = getattr(config, \"num_kv_shared_layers\", 0)\n        first_kv_shared_layer_idx = config.num_hidden_layers - num_kv_shared_layers\n        self.is_kv_shared_layer = (\n            layer_id >= first_kv_shared_layer_idx and num_kv_shared_layers > 0\n        )\n\n        self.kv_shared_layer_index = None\n        if num_kv_shared_layers > 0 and self.layer_id >= first_kv_shared_layer_idx:\n            prev_layers = config.layer_types[:first_kv_shared_layer_idx]\n            current_layer_type = config.layer_types[self.layer_id]\n            if current_layer_type not in prev_layers:\n                raise ValueError(\n                    f\"KV sharing layer {self.layer_id} has type '{current_layer_type}' \"\n                    f\"but no matching type found in layers 0..{first_kv_shared_layer_idx - 1}. \"\n                    f\"Available types: {set(prev_layers)}\"\n                )\n            self.kv_shared_layer_index = (\n                len(prev_layers) - 1 - prev_layers[::-1].index(current_layer_type)\n            )\n\n        self.rotary_emb = get_rope(\n            self.head_dim,\n            rotary_dim=self.head_dim,\n            max_position=max_position_embeddings,\n            base=rope_parameters.get(\"rope_theta\", 10000.0),\n            rope_scaling={\"rope_type\": rope_parameters.get(\"rope_type\", \"default\")},\n            partial_rotary_factor=rope_parameters.get(\"partial_rotary_factor\", 1.0),\n            is_neox_style=True,\n        )\n\n        self.attn = RadixAttention(\n            self.num_heads,\n            self.head_dim,\n            1,  # scaling factor\n            num_kv_heads=self.num_kv_heads,\n            layer_id=(\n                self.kv_shared_layer_index if self.is_kv_shared_layer else self.layer_id","lineStart":1,"lineEnd":400,"totalLines":1421,"truncated":true}},"title":"sgl-workspace/sglang/python/sglang/srt/models/gemma4_causal.py","time":{"start":1784476749345,"end":1784476749354}},"id":"prt_f7b1a4ad2001JV2HnXc5C0w7yl","sessionID":"ses_084e653e2ffeX7v9nOpFRiIG7A","messageID":"msg_f7b1a47af001hrcDPJEZZkxvNx"}}

Gemma4 residual pattern following JAX implementation:\n652:         # 1. input_norm(x) -> attn -> post_attn_norm -> ADD residual\n653:         # 2. pre_ff_norm -> mlp -> post_ff_norm -> ADD residual\n654:         #\n655:         # Optimization: fuse \"post_attn_norm(h) + residual; pre_ff_norm(...)\"\n656:         # into \"post_attn_norm(h); pre_ff_norm(h, residual)\" using\n657:         # gemma_fused_add_rmsnorm which computes:\n658:         # Combine branches\n716:             hidden_states = hidden_states_1 + hidden_states_2\n717:         else:\n718:             # Fuse: hidden_states + residual -> residual; pre_ff_norm(residual) -> hidden_states\n719:             hidden_states, residual = self.pre_feedforward_layernorm(\n720:                 hidden_states, residual\n721:             )\n722:             hidden_states = self.mlp(hidden_states)\n723: \n724:         if (\n725:             not self.has_ple\n726:             and self.moe is None\n727:             and (hidden_states.is_cuda or hidden_states.is_xpu)\n728:             and hidden_states.dim() == 2\n729:         ):\n730:             # Fused: (post_ff_norm(h) + residual) * layer_scalar in one kernel\n731:             norm = self.post_feedforward_layernorm\n732:             hidden_states = gemma_rmsnorm_residual_scalar(\n733:                 hidden_states,\n734:                 norm.weight.data,\n735:                 residual,\n736:                 self.layer_scalar,\n737:                 norm.variance_epsilon,\n738:             )\n739:         else:\n740:             hidden_states = self.post_feedforward_layernorm(hidden_states)\n741:             hidden_states = hidden_states + residual\n742: \n743:             if self.has_ple and per_layer_input is not None:\n744:                 gate, _ = self.per_layer_input_gate(hidden_states)\n745:                 gate = torch.nn.functional.gelu(gate, approximate=\"tanh\")\n746:                 gated_per_layer = gate * per_layer_input\n747:                 per_layer_contribution, _ = self.per_layer_projection(gated_per_layer)\n748:                 per_layer_contribution = self.post_per_layer_input_norm(\n749:                     per_layer_contribution\n750:                 )\n751:                 hidden_states = hidden_states + per_layer_contribution\n752: \n753:             hidden_states = hidden_states * self.layer_scalar\n754:         return hidden_states, None\n755: \n756: \n757: class Gemma4TextModel(PreTrainedModel):\n758:     def __init__(\n759:         self,\n760:         config: Gemma4TextConfig,\n761:         quant_config: Optional[QuantizationConfig] = None,\n762:         prefix: str = \"\",\n763:     ) -> None:\n764:         super().__init__(config=config)\n765:         self.config = config\n766:         self.quant_config = quant_config\n767:         self.vocab_size = config.vocab_size\n768:         self.padding_idx = getattr(config, \"pad_token_id\", None)\n769:         self.pp_group = get_pp_group()\n770: \n771:         # Token / per-layer embedding tables and the per-layer projection only\n772:         # produce activations consumed at the model entry, so they live on the\n773:         # first PP rank only.  Other ranks substitute PPMissingLayer so that\n774:         # parameter iteration still works (load_weights skips them explicitly).\n775:         self.hidden_size = config.hidden_size\n776:         self.hidden_size_per_layer_input = (\n777:             getattr(config, \"hidden_size_per_layer_input\", None) or 0\n778:         )\n779:         self.vocab_size_per_layer_input = (\n780:             getattr(config, \"vocab_size_per_layer_input\", None) or config.vocab_size\n781:         )\n782: \n783:         # PLE-enabled variants (E2B/E4B) forward `per_layer_inputs` through\n784:         # the PP proxy, but cuda_graph_runner hardcodes the proxy schema to\n785:         # {hidden_states, residual} and silently drops any extra keys at\n786:         # replay time.  Empirically this corrupts E4B output to garbage on\n787:         # non-first PP ranks (eager path produces correct output and\n788:         # GSM8K ~0.92, cuda-graph path emits token soup).  Refuse the\n789:         # combination until the runner becomes schema-aware; users can run\n790:         # PP + PLE eagerly with --disable-cuda-graph.\n791:         if self.pp_group.world_size > 1 and self.hidden_size_per_layer_input > 0:\n792:             sa = get_global_server_args()\n793:             if sa is not None and not sa.disable_cuda_graph:\n794:                 raise ValueError(\n795:                     \"Pipeline parallelism is currently incompatible with \"\n796:                     \"per-layer-input (PLE) embeddings under CUDA graph: \"\n797:                     \"the runner's PP proxy schema is hardcoded to \"\n798:                     \"{hidden_states, residual} and silently drops \"\n799:                     \"per_layer_inputs, corrupting per-layer contributions on \"\n\n(Showing lines 400-799 of 1421. Fused Q/K/V RMSNorm: replaces three separate norm kernels with one.\n        # Preconditions for the fused path: tensors on CUDA or XPU (the kernel\n        # is pure Triton and lowers to both backends), q_norm/k_norm use the\n        # standard norm*weight (scale_shift==0) and v_norm has weight=ones\n        # (with_scale=False) — the canonical Gemma4 attention configuration.\n        is_kv_shared = (\n            self.is_kv_shared_layer and self.kv_shared_layer_index is not None\n        )\n        can_fuse_qkv_norm = (\n            (q.is_cuda or q.is_xpu)\n            and self.q_norm.scale_shift == 0.0\n            and self.k_norm.scale_shift == 0.0\n            and not self.v_norm.with_scale\n        )\n        if can_fuse_qkv_norm:\n            if is_kv_shared:\n                gemma_qkv_rmsnorm(\n                    q,\n                    None,\n                    None,\n                    self.q_norm.weight.data,\n                    None,\n                    num_q_heads=self.num_heads,\n                    num_kv_heads=self.num_kv_heads,\n                    head_dim=self.head_dim,\n                    eps=self.q_norm.eps,\n                )\n                k = None\n                v = None\n            else:\n                gemma_qkv_rmsnorm(\n                    q,\n                    k,\n                    v,\n                    self.q_norm.weight.data,\n                    self.k_norm.weight.data,\n                    num_q_heads=self.num_heads,\n                    num_kv_heads=self.num_kv_heads,\n                    head_dim=self.head_dim,\n                    eps=self.q_norm.eps,\n                )\n                # Match the original norm path's output shapes: q stays 2D,\n                # k/v become 3D so the subsequent `.flatten(-2, -1)` works.\n                # Use reshape (not view) since k/v are strided slice views of\n                # the qkv buffer and may not satisfy view's contiguity rules.\n                k = k.reshape(-1, self.num_kv_heads, self.head_dim)\n                v = v.reshape(-1, self.num_kv_heads, self.head_dim)\n        else:\n            q = q.unflatten(-1, (self.num_heads, self.head_dim))\n            q = self.q_norm(q)\n            q = q.flatten(-2, -1)\n            if is_kv_shared:\n                k = None\n                v = None\n            else:\n                k = k.unflatten(-1, (self.num_kv_heads, self.head_dim))\n                k = self.k_norm(k)\n                v = v.unflatten(-1, (self.num_kv_heads, self.head_dim))\n                v = self.v_norm(v)\n\n        # Apply rotary embedding\n        use_fused_kv = False\n        if k is not None:\n            k = k.flatten(-2, -1)\n            # Fuse RoPE + KV-cache write for non-SWA layers with bf16 cache\n            # DISABLED: causes accuracy regression in launch_server path\n            can_fuse = False\n            if can_fuse:\n                fused_arg = create_fused_set_kv_buffer_arg(\n                    value=v.flatten(-2, -1) if v.dim() == 3 else v,\n                    layer=self.attn,\n                    forward_batch=forward_batch,\n                )\n                use_fused_kv = True\n            else:\n                fused_arg = None\n            q, k = self.rotary_emb(positions, q, k, fused_set_kv_buffer_arg=fused_arg)\n            k = k.unflatten(-1, (self.num_kv_heads, self.head_dim))\n        else:\n            # Rotary embedding requires a key input; use zeros since KV is shared from another layer\n            dummy_k = torch.zeros_like(q[:, : self.kv_size])\n            q, _ = self.rotary_emb(positions, q, dummy_k)\n\n        q = q.unflatten(-1, (self.num_heads, self.head_dim))\n        attn_output = self.attn(\n            q,\n            k,\n            v,\n            forward_batch=forward_batch,\n            save_kv_cache=not self.is_kv_shared_layer and not use_fused_kv,\n        )\n        if attn_output.dim() == 3:\n            attn_output = attn_output.flatten(-2, -1)\n        output, _ = self.o_proj(attn_output)\n\n        return output\n\n\nclass Gemma4DecoderLayer(nn.Module):\n    def __init__(\n        self,\n        layer_id: int,\n        config: PretrainedConfig,\n        quant_config: Optional[QuantizationConfig] = None,\n        prefix: str = \"\",\n    ) -> None:\n        super().__init__()\n        self.hidden_size = config.hidden_size\n        self.hidden_size_per_layer_input = (\n            getattr(config, \"hidden_size_per_layer_input\", None) or 0\n        )\n\n        self.layer_id = layer_id\n\n        # Gemma 4 uses different head dimensions for sliding vs full attention\n        layer_type = config.layer_types[layer_id]\n        self.is_full_attention = layer_type == \"full_attention\"\n        if self.is_full_attention:\n            head_dim = config.head_dim  # following sglang naming\n        else:\n            head_dim = getattr(config, \"swa_head_dim\", config.head_dim)\n\n        self.self_attn = Gemma4Attention(\n            layer_id=layer_id,\n            config=config,\n            max_position_embeddings=config.max_position_embeddings,\n            head_dim=head_dim,\n            quant_config=quant_config,\n            prefix=add_prefix(\"self_attn\", prefix),\n        )\n\n        first_kv_shared_layer_idx = config.num_hidden_layers - getattr(\n            config, \"num_kv_shared_layers\", 0\n        )\n        is_kv_shared_layer = self.layer_id >= first_kv_shared_layer_idx > 0\n        use_double_wide_mlp = (\n            getattr(config, \"use_double_wide_mlp\", False) and is_kv_shared_layer\n        )\n        layer_intermediate_size = config.intermediate_size * (\n            2 if use_double_wide_mlp else 1\n        )\n\n        self.mlp = Gemma4MLP(\n            hidden_size=self.hidden_size,\n            intermediate_size=layer_intermediate_size,\n            hidden_activation=config.hidden_activation,\n            quant_config=quant_config,\n            prefix=add_prefix(\"mlp\", prefix),\n        )\n\n        self.input_layernorm = RMSNorm(config.hidden_size, eps=config.rms_norm_eps)\n        self.post_attention_layernorm = RMSNorm(\n            config.hidden_size, eps=config.rms_norm_eps\n        )\n        self.pre_feedforward_layernorm = RMSNorm(\n            config.hidden_size, eps=config.rms_norm_eps\n        )\n        self.post_feedforward_layernorm = RMSNorm(\n            config.hidden_size, eps=config.rms_norm_eps\n        )\n\n        # Per-Layer Embedding (PLE) components — present in each decoder layer\n        if self.hidden_size_per_layer_input > 0:\n            # Gate: projects hidden_states → per-layer dim for gating\n            self.per_layer_input_gate = ReplicatedLinear(\n                self.hidden_size,\n                self.hidden_size_per_layer_input,\n                bias=False,\n                quant_config=quant_config,\n                prefix=add_prefix(\"per_layer_input_gate\", prefix),\n            )\n            # Projection: projects gated per-layer input back → hidden size\n            self.per_layer_projection = ReplicatedLinear(\n                self.hidden_size_per_layer_input,\n                self.hidden_size,\n                bias=False,\n                quant_config=quant_config,\n                prefix=add_prefix(\"per_layer_projection\", prefix),\n            )\n            self.post_per_layer_input_norm = Gemma4RMSNorm(\n                config.hidden_size, eps=config.rms_norm_eps\n            )\n        else:\n            self.per_layer_input_gate = None\n            self.per_layer_projection = None\n            self.post_per_layer_input_norm = None\n\n        # Parallel MoE\n        self.enable_moe_block = getattr(config, \"enable_moe_block\", False)\n        if self.enable_moe_block:\n            self.router = Gemma4Router(\n                config,\n                quant_config=quant_config,\n                prefix=add_prefix(\"router\", prefix),\n            )\n            self.moe = Gemma4MoE(\n                hidden_size=self.hidden_size,\n                layer_id=layer_id,\n                config=config,\n                quant_config=quant_config,\n                prefix=add_prefix(\"moe\", prefix),\n            )\n\n            self.post_feedforward_layernorm_1 = RMSNorm(\n                config.hidden_size, eps=config.rms_norm_eps\n            )\n            self.post_feedforward_layernorm_2 = RMSNorm(\n                config.hidden_size, eps=config.rms_norm_eps\n            )\n            self.pre_feedforward_layernorm_2 = RMSNorm(\n                config.hidden_size, eps=config.rms_norm_eps\n            )\n        else:\n            self.router = None\n            self.moe = None\n            self.post_feedforward_layernorm_1 = None\n            self.post_feedforward_layernorm_2 = None\n            self.pre_feedforward_layernorm_2 = None\n\n        self.register_buffer(\"layer_scalar\", torch.ones(1), persistent=True)\n        self.has_ple = self.hidden_size_per_layer_input > 0\n        self.prefix = prefix\n\n    def forward(\n        self,\n        positions: torch.Tensor,\n        hidden_states: torch.Tensor,\n        per_layer_input: torch.Tensor,\n        forward_batch: ForwardBatch,\n        **kwargs,\n    ) -> tuple[\n        torch.FloatTensor, Optional[tuple[torch.FloatTensor, torch.FloatTensor]]\n    ]:\n        # Gemma4 residual pattern following JAX implementation:\n        # 1. input_norm(x) -> attn -> post_attn_norm -> ADD residual\n        # 2. pre_ff_norm -> mlp -> post_ff_norm -> ADD residual\n        #\n        # Optimization: fuse \"post_attn_norm(h) + residual; pre_ff_norm(...)\"\n        # into \"post_attn_norm(h); pre_ff_norm(h, residual)\" using\n        # gemma_fused_add_rmsnorm which computes:\n        #   residual = h + residual (in-place)\n        #   h = gemma_norm(residual)\n        residual = hidden_states\n\n        # Apply input layernorm\n        hidden_states = self.input_layernorm(hidden_states)\n        hidden_states = self.self_attn(\n            positions=positions,\n            hidden_states=hidden_states,\n            forward_batch=forward_batch,\n        )\n        hidden_states = self.post_attention_layernorm(hidden_states)\n\n        if self.enable_moe_block:\n            # Fuse: hidden_states + residual -> residual; pre_ff_norm(residual) -> hidden_states\n            # Also need raw (unfused) residual for router and pre_ff_norm_2\n            hidden_states, residual = self.pre_feedforward_layernorm(\n                hidden_states, residual\n            )\n            # For MoE: router and pre_ff_norm_2 need the unfused residual\n            # (which is now updated to post_attn_out + old_residual)\n            moe_input = residual\n\n            # Dense MLP branch\n            hidden_states_1 = self.mlp(hidden_states)\n\n            # MoE branch: router sees residual (= post_attn_out + old_residual)\n            router_logits = self.router(moe_input)\n            hidden_states_2 = self.pre_feedforward_layernorm_2(moe_input)\n            hidden_states_2 = self.moe(hidden_states_2, router_logits)\n\n            # Fused: (rmsnorm(rmsnorm(h1,w1) + rmsnorm(h2,w2), w3) + residual) * scalar\n            if (\n                not self.has_ple\n                and (hidden_states_1.is_cuda or hidden_states_1.is_xpu)\n                and hidden_states_1.dim() == 2\n            ):\n                norm1 = self.post_feedforward_layernorm_1\n                norm2 = self.post_feedforward_layernorm_2\n                norm3 = self.post_feedforward_layernorm\n                hidden_states = gemma_dual_rmsnorm_residual_scalar(\n                    hidden_states_1,\n                    norm1.weight.data,\n                    hidden_states_2,\n                    norm2.weight.data,\n                    norm3.weight.data,\n                    residual,\n                    self.layer_scalar,\n                    norm1.variance_epsilon,\n                    norm2.variance_epsilon,\n                    norm3.variance_epsilon,\n                )\n                return hidden_states, None\n\n            hidden_states_1 = self.post_feedforward_layernorm_1(hidden_states_1)\n            hidden_states_2 = self.post_feedforward_layernorm_2(hidden_states_2)\n\n            # Combine branches\n            hidden_states = hidden_states_1 + hidden_states_2\n        else:\n            # Fuse: hidden_states + residual -> residual; pre_ff_norm(residual) -> hidden_states\n            hidden_states, residual = self.pre_feedforward_layernorm(\n                hidden_states, residual\n            )\n            hidden_states = self.mlp(hidden_states)\n\n        if (\n            not self.has_ple\n            and self.moe is None\n            and (hidden_states.is_cuda or hidden_states.is_xpu)\n            and hidden_states.dim() == 2\n        ):\n            # Fused: (post_ff_norm(h) + residual) * layer_scalar in one kernel\n            norm = self.post_feedforward_layernorm\n            hidden_states = gemma_rmsnorm_residual_scalar(\n                hidden_states,\n                norm.weight.data,\n                residual,\n                self.layer_scalar,\n                norm.variance_epsilon,\n            )\n        else:\n            hidden_states = self.post_feedforward_layernorm(hidden_states)\n            hidden_states = hidden_states + residual\n\n            if self.has_ple and per_layer_input is not None:\n                gate, _ = self.per_layer_input_gate(hidden_states)\n                gate = torch.nn.functional.gelu(gate, approximate=\"tanh\")\n                gated_per_layer = gate * per_layer_input\n                per_layer_contribution, _ = self.per_layer_projection(gated_per_layer)\n                per_layer_contribution = self.post_per_layer_input_norm(\n                    per_layer_contribution\n                )\n                hidden_states = hidden_states + per_layer_contribution\n\n            hidden_states = hidden_states * self.layer_scalar\n        return hidden_states, None\n\n\nclass Gemma4TextModel(PreTrainedModel):\n    def __init__(\n        self,\n        config: Gemma4TextConfig,\n        quant_config: Optional[QuantizationConfig] = None,\n        prefix: str = \"\",\n    ) -> None:\n        super().__init__(config=config)\n        self.config = config\n        self.quant_config = quant_config\n        self.vocab_size = config.vocab_size\n        self.padding_idx = getattr(config, \"pad_token_id\", None)\n        self.pp_group = get_pp_group()\n\n        # Token / per-layer embedding tables and the per-layer projection only\n        # produce activations consumed at the model entry, so they live on the\n        # first PP rank only.  Other ranks substitute PPMissingLayer so that\n        # parameter iteration still works (load_weights skips them explicitly).\n        self.hidden_size = config.hidden_size\n        self.hidden_size_per_layer_input = (\n            getattr(config, \"hidden_size_per_layer_input\", None) or 0\n        )\n        self.vocab_size_per_layer_input = (\n            getattr(config, \"vocab_size_per_layer_input\", None) or config.vocab_size\n        )\n\n        # PLE-enabled variants (E2B/E4B) forward `per_layer_inputs` through\n        # the PP proxy, but cuda_graph_runner hardcodes the proxy schema to\n        # {hidden_states, residual} and silently drops any extra keys at\n        # replay time.  Empirically this corrupts E4B output to garbage on\n        # non-first PP ranks (eager path produces correct output and\n        # GSM8K ~0.92, cuda-graph path emits token soup).  Refuse the\n        # combination until the runner becomes schema-aware; users can run\n        # PP + PLE eagerly with --disable-cuda-graph.\n        if self.pp_group.world_size > 1 and self.hidden_size_per_layer_input > 0:\n            sa = get_global_server_args()\n            if sa is not None and not sa.disable_cuda_graph:\n                raise ValueError(\n                    \"Pipeline parallelism is currently incompatible with \"\n                    \"per-layer-input (PLE) embeddings under CUDA graph: \"\n                    \"the runner's PP proxy schema is hardcoded to \"\n                    \"{hidden_states, residual} and silently drops \"\n                    \"per_layer_inputs, corrupting per-layer contributions on \"","lineStart":400,"lineEnd":799,"totalLines":1421,"truncated":true}},"title":"sgl-workspace/sglang/python/sglang/srt/models/gemma4_causal.py","time":{"start":1784476751232,"end":1784476751239}},"id":"prt_f7b1a5193001nIo8gwVh3ly4LZ","sessionID":"ses_084e653e2ffeX7v9nOpFRiIG7A","messageID":"msg_f7b1a4e78001bUt4Apt2zMP6SB"}}

next(self.parameters()).dtype\n875: \n876:     def get_per_layer_inputs(self, input_ids: torch.LongTensor) -> torch.Tensor:\n877:         if self.embed_tokens_per_layer is None:\n878:             return None\n879: \n880:         # Handle out-of-vocab tokens for PLE (vocab_size_per_layer_input may\n881:         # be smaller than the main vocab_size). Following Gemma3n pattern.\n882:         per_layer_inputs_mask = torch.logical_and(\n883:             input_ids >= 0,\n884:             input_ids < self.vocab_size_per_layer_input,\n885:         )\n886:         per_layer_inputs_tokens = torch.where(\n887:             per_layer_inputs_mask, input_ids, torch.zeros_like(input_ids)\n888:         )\n889: \n890:         # Get packed per-layer embeddings: (num_tokens, total_ple_dim)\n891:         per_layer_embeds = self.embed_tokens_per_layer(per_layer_inputs_tokens)\n892: \n893:         # Apply embed_scale (sqrt of per-layer hidden dim)\n894:         # Already done in embedding layer\n895:         # per_layer_embeds = per_layer_embeds * self.embed_scale_per_layer\n896: \n897:         # Reshape to (num_tokens, num_layers, hidden_size_per_layer_input)\n898:         per_layer_embeds = per_layer_embeds.reshape(\n899:             *input_ids.shape,\n900:             self.config.num_hidden_layers,\n901:             self.hidden_size_per_layer_input,\n902:         )\n903:         return per_layer_embeds\n904: \n905:     def project_per_layer_inputs(\n906:         self,\n907:         inputs_embeds: torch.Tensor,\n908:         per_layer_inputs: Optional[torch.Tensor] = None,\n909:     ) -> torch.Tensor:\n910:         \"\"\"Project inputs_embeds and combine with per_layer_inputs.\n911: \n912:         Following HF/Gemma3n reference:\n913:         1. )\n\n        if self.pp_group.is_first_rank:\n            self.embed_tokens = Gemma4TextScaledWordEmbedding(\n                config.vocab_size,\n                config.hidden_size,\n                self.padding_idx,\n                embed_scale=self.config.hidden_size**0.5,  # embedded normalizer\n            )\n        else:\n            self.embed_tokens = PPMissingLayer()\n\n        if (\n            self.pp_group.is_first_rank\n            and self.hidden_size_per_layer_input\n            and self.hidden_size_per_layer_input > 0\n        ):\n            self.embed_tokens_per_layer = Gemma4TextScaledWordEmbedding(\n                self.vocab_size_per_layer_input,\n                config.num_hidden_layers * self.hidden_size_per_layer_input,\n                self.padding_idx,\n config.num_hidden_layers * self.hidden_size_per_layer_input,\n                bias=False,\n                quant_config=quant_config,\n                prefix=add_prefix(\"per_layer_model_projection\", prefix),\n            )\n\n            self.per_layer_projection_norm = RMSNorm(\n                self.hidden_size_per_layer_input,\n                config.rms_norm_eps,\n            )\n            self.per_layer_input_scale = torch.rsqrt(torch.tensor(2.0))\n            self.per_layer_projection_scale = torch.tensor(\n                config.hidden_size**-0.5,\n            )\n        else:\n    self.per_layer_projection_scale = None\n\n        self.layers, self.start_layer, self.end_layer = make_layers(\n            config.num_hidden_layers,\n            lambda idx, prefix: Gemma4DecoderLayer(\n                layer_id=idx,\n                config=config,\n                quant_config=quant_config,\n                prefix=prefix,\n            ),\n get_input_embeddings(self) -> nn.Embedding:\n        return self.embed_tokens\n\n    def dtype(self) -> torch.dtype:\n        return next(self.parameters()).dtype\n\n    def get_per_layer_inputs(self, input_ids: torch.LongTensor) -> torch.Tensor:\n        if self.embed_tokens_per_layer is None:\n            return None\n\n        # Handle out-of-vocab tokens for PLE (vocab_size_per_layer_input may\n        # be smaller than the main vocab_size). Following Gemma3n pattern.\n        per_layer_inputs_mask = torch.logical_and(\n            input_ids >= 0,\n            input_ids < self.vocab_size_per_layer_input,\n        )\n        per_layer_inputs_tokens = torch.where(\n            per_layer_inputs_mask, input_ids, torch.zeros_like(input_ids)\n        )\n\n        # Get packed per-layer embeddings: (num_tokens, total_ple_dim)\n        per_layer_embeds = self.embed_tokens_per_layer(per_layer_inputs_tokens)\n\n        # Apply embed_scale (sqrt of per-layer hidden dim)\n        # Already done in embedding layer\n        # per_layer_embeds = per_layer_embeds * self.embed_scale_per_layer\n\n        # Reshape to (num_tokens, num_layers, hidden_size_per_layer_input)\n        per_layer_embeds = per_layer_embeds.reshape(\n            *input_ids.shape,\n            self.config.num_hidden_layers,\n            self.hidden_size_per_layer_input,\n        )\n        return per_layer_embeds\n\n    def project_per_layer_inputs(\n        self,\n        inputs_embeds: torch.Tensor,\n        per_layer_inputs: Optional[torch.Tensor] = None,\n    ) -> torch.Tensor:\n        \"\"\"Project inputs_embeds and combine with per_layer_inputs.\n\n        Following HF/Gemma3n reference:\n        1. Project inputs_embeds: hidden_size → total_ple_dim\n        2. Scale by hidden_size^{-0.5} (Gemma4ScaledLinear w_scale)\n        3. Reshape to (num_tokens, num_layers, per_layer_dim)\n        4. Normalize with per_layer_projection_norm\n        5. Combine: (projection + per_layer_inputs) * 1/sqrt(2)\n        \"\"\"\n        if self.per_layer_model_projection is None:\n            return None\n\n        # Project from hidden_size to total_ple_dim\n        per_layer_projection, _ = self.per_layer_model_projection(inputs_embeds)\n\n        # Apply w_scale (HF: Gemma4ScaledLinear with w_scale=hidden_size^{-0.5})\n        per_layer_projection = per_layer_projection * self.per_layer_projection_scale\n\n        # Reshape to (num_tokens, num_layers, hidden_size_per_layer_input)\n        per_layer_projection = per_layer_projection.reshape(\n            *inputs_embeds.shape[:-1],\n            self.config.num_hidden_layers,\n            self.hidden_size_per_layer_input,\n        )\n\n        # Normalize\n        per_layer_projection = self.per_layer_projection_norm(per_layer_projection)\n\n        if per_layer_inputs is None:\n            return per_layer_projection\n\n        # Combine: (projection + per_layer_inputs) * scale\n        return (per_layer_projection + per_layer_inputs) * self.per_layer_input_scale\n\n    def forward(\n        self,\n        input_ids: torch.Tensor,\n        positions: torch.Tensor,\n        forward_batch: ForwardBatch,\n        input_embeds: torch.Tensor = None,\n        per_layer_inputs: Optional[torch.Tensor] = None,\n        pp_proxy_tensors: Optional[PPProxyTensors] = None,\n        **kwargs,\n    ) -> Union[torch.Tensor, Tuple[torch.Tensor, List[torch.Tensor]], PPProxyTensors]:\n        if self.pp_group.is_first_rank:\n            if (input_ids is None) ^ (input_embeds is not None):\n                raise ValueError(\n                    \"You must specify exactly one of input_ids or inputs_embeds\"\n                )\n\n            if input_ids is not None:\n                input_embeds = self.embed_tokens(input_ids)\n                per_layer_inputs = self.get_per_layer_inputs(input_ids)\n            per_layer_inputs = self.project_per_layer_inputs(\n                input_embeds, per_layer_inputs\n            )\n            hidden_states = input_embeds\n        else:\n            assert (\n                pp_proxy_tensors is not None\n            ), \"pp_proxy_tensors is required on non-first PP ranks\"\n            hidden_states = pp_proxy_tensors[\"hidden_states\"]\n            # PLE inputs were computed on rank 0 and forwarded along the\n            # pipeline; non-PLE models simply omit the key.\n            per_layer_inputs = pp_proxy_tensors.tensors.get(\"per_layer_inputs\", None)\n\n        aux_hidden_states = []\n        num_layers = self.config.num_hidden_layers\n\n        for layer_idx in range(self.start_layer, self.end_layer):\n            if layer_idx in self.layers_to_capture:\n                aux_hidden_states.append(hidden_states)\n\n            if per_layer_inputs is not None:\n                per_layer_input = per_layer_inputs[:, layer_idx, :]\n            else:\n                per_layer_input = None\n            layer = self.layers[layer_idx]\n            layer_outputs = layer(\n                positions=positions,\n                hidden_states=hidden_states,\n                per_layer_input=per_layer_input,\n                forward_batch=forward_batch,\n                **kwargs,\n            )\n            hidden_states = layer_outputs[0]\n            # Gemma4DecoderLayer.forward always returns (hidden_states, None);\n            # the residual is fused inside the layer, so nothing to thread.\n\n        if not self.pp_group.is_last_rank:\n            # cuda_graph_runner allocates a fixed PP-proxy schema of\n            # {hidden_states, residual} and KeyErrors if a model omits a key.\n            # Gemma4 fuses the residual inside each layer so we don't have a\n            # standalone tensor to forward; emit a zero placeholder instead so\n            # graph replay can still copy it.  The receiving stage never reads\n            # this key.\n            proxy = {\n                \"hidden_states\": hidden_states,\n                \"residual\": torch.zeros_like(hidden_states),\n            }\n            if per_layer_inputs is not None:\n                proxy[\"per_layer_inputs\"] = per_layer_inputs\n            return PPProxyTensors(proxy)\n\n        # Capture the output of the last layer if requested.\n        # layers_to_capture uses +1 offset, so num_layers means\n        # \"output of the last layer\" which is only available after the loop.\n        if num_layers in self.layers_to_capture:\n            aux_hidden_states.append(hidden_states)\n\n        hidden_states = self.norm(hidden_states)\n\n        if len(aux_hidden_states) == 0:\n            return hidden_states\n\n        return hidden_states, aux_hidden_states\n\n\nclass Gemma4ForCausalLM(PreTrainedModel):\n    config_class = Gemma4TextConfig\n    base_model_prefix = \"language_model\"\n    _tied_weights_keys = {\"lm_head.weight\": \"model.embed_tokens.weight\"}\n    _tp_plan = {\"lm_head\": \"colwise_rep\"}\n\n    # BitandBytes specific attributes\n    default_bitsandbytes_target_modules = [\n        \".gate_proj.\",\n        \".down_proj.\",\n        \".up_proj.\",\n        \".q_proj.\",\n        \".k_proj.\",\n        \".v_proj.\",\n        \".o_proj.\",\n    ]\n    bitsandbytes_stacked_params_mapping = {\n        # shard_name, weight_name, index\n        \"q_proj\": (\"qkv_proj\", 0),\n        \"k_proj\": (\"qkv_proj\", 1),\n        \"v_proj\": (\"qkv_proj\", 2),\n        \"gate_proj\": (\"gate_up_proj\", 0),\n        \"up_proj\": (\"gate_up_proj\", 1),\n    }\n\n    packed_modules_mapping = {\n        \"qkv_proj\": [\n            \"q_proj\",\n            \"k_proj\",\n            \"v_proj\",\n        ],\n        \"gate_up_proj\": [\n            \"gate_proj\",\n            \"up_proj\",\n        ],\n    }\n\n    # Gemma does not apply LoRA to the embedding layer.\n    embedding_modules = {}\n    embedding_padding_modules = []\n    supports_lora = False\n\n    def __init__(\n        self,\n        config: Gemma4TextConfig,\n        quant_config: Optional[QuantizationConfig] = None,\n        prefix: str = \"\",\n    ) -> None:\n        super().__init__(config=config)\n        self.pp_group = get_pp_group()\n        self.config = config\n        self.quant_config = quant_config\n\n        self.model = Gemma4TextModel(\n            config=config, quant_config=quant_config, prefix=add_prefix(\"model\", prefix)\n        )\n        self.logits_processor = LogitsProcessor(config)\n\n        # tie_word_embeddings ties lm_head to embed_tokens, but with PP those\n        # tensors live on opposite ranks (first vs last).  In the PP > 1 case\n        # we materialize a real ParallelLMHead on the last rank and route the\n        # checkpoint's embed_tokens.weight into it during load_weights.\n        if self.pp_group.world_size == 1 and self.config.tie_word_embeddings:\n            self.lm_head = self.model.embed_tokens\n        elif self.pp_group.is_last_rank:\n            self.lm_head = ParallelLMHead(\n                config.vocab_size,\n                config.hidden_size,\n                quant_config=quant_config,\n                prefix=add_prefix(\"lm_head\", prefix),\n            )\n        else:\n            self.lm_head = PPMissingLayer()\n\n        self.capture_aux_hidden_states = False\n        self.post_init()\n\n    def tie_weights(self, *args, **kwargs):\n        # HF's PreTrainedModel.tie_weights uses ``_tied_weights_keys`` to bind\n        # ``lm_head.weight`` to ``model.embed_tokens.weight``.  Under PP those\n        # tensors live on different ranks (embed on first, head on last) and\n        # the missing side is a PPMissingLayer with no ``weight`` attribute,\n        # which makes the default tie_weights crash.  load_weights routes the\n        # checkpoint embedding into lm_head explicitly, so the tie is a no-op\n        # here when PP is active.\n        if self.pp_group.world_size > 1:\n            return\n        super().tie_weights(*args, **kwargs)\n\n    def get_input_embeddings(self) -> nn.Embedding:\n        return self.model.embed_tokens\n\n    def get_embed_and_head(self) -> Tuple[torch.Tensor, torch.Tensor]:\n        return self.model.embed_tokens.weight, self.lm_head.weight\n\n    def get_attention_sliding_window_size(self):\n        return get_attention_sliding_window_size(self.config)\n\n    def dtype(self) -> torch.dtype:\n        return next(self.parameters()).dtype\n\n    def set_dflash_layers_to_capture(self, layer_ids: list[int]):\n        if layer_ids is None:\n            raise ValueError(\n                \"DFLASH requires explicit layer_ids for aux hidden capture.\"\n            )\n        self.capture_aux_hidden_states = True\n        self.model.layers_to_capture = [val + 1 for val in layer_ids]\n\n    @torch.no_grad()\n    def forward(\n        self,\n        input_ids: torch.Tensor,\n        positions: torch.Tensor,\n        forward_batch: ForwardBatch,\n        input_embeds: torch.Tensor = None,\n        per_layer_inputs: Optional[torch.Tensor] = None,\n        pp_proxy_tensors: Optional[PPProxyTensors] = None,\n        **kwargs,\n    ) -> Union[LogitsProcessor, PPProxyTensors]:\n        hidden_states = self.model(\n            input_ids,\n            positions,\n            forward_batch,\n            input_embeds,\n            per_layer_inputs,\n            pp_proxy_tensors=pp_proxy_tensors,\n            **kwargs,\n        )\n\n        if not self.pp_group.is_last_rank:\n            # `hidden_states` here is actually a PPProxyTensors handed off to\n            # the next stage; logits processing only happens on the last rank.\n            return hidden_states\n\n        aux_hidden_states = None\n        if self.capture_aux_hidden_states:\n            hidden_states, aux_hidden_states = hidden_states\n\n        return self.logits_processor(\n            input_ids, hidden_states, self.lm_head, forward_batch, aux_hidden_states\n        )\n\n    def _get_k_eq_v_layers(self) -> set:\n        \"\"\"Return set of layer indices where attention_k_eq_v applies (full-attention layers).\"\"\"\n        if not getattr(self.config, \"attention_k_eq_v\", False):\n            return set()\n        return {\n            i for i, lt in enumerate(self.config.layer_types) if lt == \"full_attention\"\n        }\n\n    def load_weights(self, weights: Iterable[Tuple[str, torch.Tensor]]):\n        stacked_params_mapping = [\n            # (param_name, shard_name, shard_id)\n            (\"qkv_proj\", \"q_proj\", \"q\"),\n            (\"qkv_proj\", \"k_proj\", \"k\"),\n            (\"qkv_proj\", \"v_proj\", \"v\"),\n            (\"gate_up_proj\", \"gate_proj\", 0),\n            (\"gate_up_proj\", \"up_proj\", 1),\n        ]\n\n        fused_expert_params_mapping = [\n            # (param_name, ckpt_weight_name, shard_ids)\n            # gate_up_proj is fused [E, 2*I, H] — chunk into w1 (gate) + w3 (up)\n            (\"experts.w13_weight\", \"experts.gate_up_proj\", (\"w1\", \"w3\")),\n            (\"experts.w2_weight\", \"experts.down_proj\", (\"w2\",)),\n        ]\n        # runner.server_args.attention_backend == \"flashinfer\"\n                ), \"triton, trtllm_mha, fa4, or flashinfer backend are the only supported backends on Blackwell GPUs for hybrid GDN models, use
