backend is the only supported backend on NPU for hybrid GDN models, use --attention-backend ascend to specify the backend.\"\n            logger.info(f\"Using hybrid linear attention backend for Short-conv hybrids (ZAYA1 CCA, LFM2 short conv) share a conv-state\n            # sidecar that owns the per-request state plumbing and is invoked by\n            # the model via conv_state_metadata (never as a full-vs-linear\n            # alternative). Other mamba2 models keep the full Mamba2 SSM backend.\n            short_conv_cfgs = (\n                ZayaConfig,\n                Lfm2Config,\n                Lfm2MoeConfig,\n                Lfm2VlConfig,\n            )\n            if isinstance(runner.mamba2_config, short_conv_cfgs):\n                if is_npu():\n                    # The model conv layers call\n                    # get_attn_backend().conv_state_metadata() unconditionally,\n                    # but the Ascend hybrid/mamba backend has no such method.\n                    # Fail here (before model execution) with a clear message\n                    # rather than an AttributeError deep in the first conv layer.\n                    raise NotImplementedError(\n                        \"Short-conv hybrid models (ZAYA1 CCA, LFM2 / LFM2-MoE) \"\n                        \"are not yet supported on NPU: the conv-state sidecar \"\n                        \"(ShortConvAttnBackend.conv_state_metadata) has no Ascend \"\n                        \"implementation. Add an Ascend conv-state backend before \"\n                        \"serving these models on NPU.\"\n                    )\n                from sglang.srt.layers.attention.hybrid_linear_attn_backend import (\n                    ShortConvHybridAttnBackend,\n                )\n                from sglang.srt.layers.attention.linear.short_conv_backend import (\n                    ShortConvAttnBackend,\n                )\n\n                linear_attn_backend = ShortConvAttnBackend(runner)\n                hybrid_backend_cls = ShortConvHybridAttnBackend\n            else:\n                linear_attn_backend = Mamba2AttnBackend(runner)\n        elif runner.kimi_linear_config is not None:\n            linear_attn_backend = KDAAttnBackend(runner)\n        elif runner.hybrid_lightning_config is not None:\n            linear_attn_backend = LightningAttentionBackend(runner)\n        else:\n            spec_result = get_linear_attn_config(runner.model_config.hf_config)\n            if spec_result is not None:\n                spec, _ = spec_result\n                BackendClass = import_backend_class(spec.backend_class_name)\n                linear_attn_backend = BackendClass(runner)\n            else:\n                raise ValueError(\n                    \"Expected hybrid GDN or NemotronH models, but got unknown model. \"\n                    \"If this is a custom hybrid model, use register_linear_attn_model() \"\n                    \"from sglang.srt.configs.linear_attn_model_registry.\"\n                )\n        if runner.is_draft_worker:\n            # FIXME: we assume that MTP/NEXTN always use full-attention.\n            full_attn_layers = [0]\n        else:\n            full_attn_layers = cfg.full_attention_layer_ids\n        return hybrid_backend_cls(\n            full_attn_backend, linear_attn_backend, full_attn_layers\n        )\n\n    return full_attn_backend\n\n\n@register_attention_backend(\"intel_xpu\")\ndef create_intel_xpu_backend(runner):\n    from sglang.srt.layers.attention.xpu_backend import XPUAttentionBackend\n\n    return XPUAttentionBackend(runner)","lineStart":1,"lineEnd":379,"totalLines":379,"truncated":false}},"title":"sgl-workspace/sglang/python/sglang/srt/layers/attention/attention_registry.py","time":{"start":1784476760900,"end":1784476760911}},"id":"prt_f7b1a78ac001QRHiy92TGxLM3n","sessionID":"ses_084e653e2ffeX7v9nOpFRiIG7A","messageID":"msg_f7b1a758a001tcORCZoyg2GGXR"}}

defaults.\n        Precedence (highest first): explicit JSON > convenience > legacy > defaults.\n        Also populates self._cuda_graph_config_locked — the set of\n        (phase, key) tuples that came from non-default sources; the\n        auto-disable cascade respects this lock (the old\n        --enforce-piecewise-cuda-graph semantics generalized).\n        \"\"\"\n        raw_input = locked.add((phase, key))\n\n        # ---- Legacy global flags (lowest precedence above defaults) ----\n        if self.disable_cuda_graph:\n            _set(Phase.DECODE, \"backend\", Backend.DISABLED)\n            _set(Phase.PREFILL, \"backend\", Backend.DISABLED)\n\n        # ---- Boolean per-phase off-switches ----\n        # Below the explicit backend selectors so --cuda-graph-backend-*\n        # wins if both are given.\n        if self.disable_prefill_cuda_graph:\n            _set(Phase.PREFILL, \"backend\", Backend.DISABLED)\n        if None:\n            _set(Phase.DECODE, \"backend\", self.cuda_graph_backend_decode)\n        if self.cuda_graph_backend_prefill is not None:\n            _set(Phase.PREFILL, \"backend\", self.cuda_graph_backend_prefill)\n        if self.cuda_graph_max_bs_decode is not None:\n            _set(Phase.DECODE, \"max_bs\", self.cuda_graph_max_bs_decode)\n        if _set(Phase.DECODE, \"bs\", self.cuda_graph_bs_decode)\n        if self.cuda_graph_bs_prefill is not None:\n            _set(Phase.PREFILL, \"bs\", self.cuda_graph_bs_prefill)\n        if self.cuda_graph_tc_compiler is not None:\n            # Written to both phases so the value is in place when TC_PIECEWISE\n            # decode is implemented; today decode ignores it.\n            _set(Phase.DECODE, \"tc_compiler\", self.cuda_graph_tc_compiler)\n            _set(Phase.PREFILL, \"tc_compiler\", self.cuda_graph_tc_compiler)\n\n        # ---- Explicit JSON config (highest _apply_cuda_graph_compatibility(self):\n        \"\"\"Auto-disable prefill cuda graph for incompatible configs.\n        Rules are split per backend — TcPiecewise and Breakable have\n        different constraints. Skipped when the user explicitly set the\n        prefill backend (this folds in the old\n        --enforce-piecewise-cuda-graph contract).\n        \"\"\"\n        if (Phase.PREFILL, \"backend\") in self._cuda_graph_config_locked:\n            return\n        if self.cuda_graph_config.prefill.backend == Backend.TC_PIECEWISE:\n            self._disable_tc_piecewise_cudagraph_if_incompatible()\n        elif self.cuda_graph_config.prefill.backend == Backend.BREAKABLE:\n            self._disable_breakable_cudagraph_if_incompatible()\n        elif self.cuda_graph_config.prefill.backend == Backend.FULL:\n            self._disable_full_prefill_cudagraph_if_incompatible()\n\n    def _disable_tc_piecewise_cudagraph_if_incompatible(self):\n        from sglang.srt.arg_groups.overrides import resolved_view as _resolved_view\n\n        \"\"\"TcPiecewise (torch.compile + piecewise) is incompatible with\n        these configurations. cache\",\n                lambda: self.cpu_offload_gb > 0 or self.enable_hierarchical_cache,\n            ),\n            (\n                \"deterministic inference\",\n                lambda: Note: flashinfer 0.6.1 caused performance regression on Hopper attention kernel\n                # Before the kernel is fixed, we choose fa3 as the default backend on Hopper MHA\n                # ref: https://github.com/sgl-project/sglang/issues/17411\n                return \"fa3\"\n            elif (\n                is_sm100_supported()\n                and is_no_spec_infer_or_topk_one(resolved_view(self))\n                and (\n                    self.speculative_algorithm is None\n                    or self.speculative_eagle_topk is not None\n                )\n            ):\n                return \"trtllm_mha\"\n            elif is_hip():\n                return \"aiter\"\n            elif is_mps():\n                return \"torch_native\"\n            else:\n                # The attention_backend write clusters of this handler moved to the\n        # resolution pipeline (arg_groups/overrides.py), each invoked below at\n        # its legacy slot; the interleaved non-attention adjustments stay.\n        from sglang.srt.arg_groups.overrides import (\n            _attention_backend_default,\n            _attention_backend_dual_chunk,\n            _attention_backend_fa3_fp8_fallback,\n            _attention_backend_platform_fallbacks,\n            _fa4_page_constraint,\n            _intel_xpu_page_constraint,\n            _mla_backend_page_constraints,\n            resolved_view,\n            run_post_process_pass,\n        )\n\n        # Whisper's encoder token padding conflicts with prefix caching.\n        # Only disable for Whisper; other encoder-decoder models (e.g., mllama) use radix cache.\n        if (\n            model_config.is_encoder_decoder\n            and not self.disable_radix_cache\n            and \"WhisperForConditionalGeneration\"\n            in (model_config.hf_config.architectures or [])\n        ):\n            logger.info(\"Radix cache is disabled for Whisper\")\n            self.disable_radix_cache = True\n\n        # _mla_kv_cache_dtype_checks), invoked here at their legacy slot.\n        from sglang.srt.arg_groups.overrides import _mla_kv_cache_dtype_checks\n\n        run_post_process_pass(self, _mla_kv_cache_dtype_checks)\n\n        # The CuteDSL MLA validation + prefill fill moved to the resolution\n        # pipeline (arg_groups/overrides.py: _cutedsl_prefill_backend_fill),\n        # invoked here at its legacy slot.\n        from sglang.srt.arg_groups.overrides import _cutedsl_prefill_backend_fill\n\n        run_post_process_pass(self, _cutedsl_prefill_backend_fill)\n\n        prefill_backend, decode_backend = self._resolved_attention_backends()\n        if \"trtllm_mha\" in (prefill_backend, decode_backend):\n            if prefill_backend == \"trtllm_mha\" and not is_sm100_supported():\n                raise ValueError(\n                    \"TRTLLM MHA backend for prefill is only supported on Blackwell GPUs (SM100). Please use a different prefill backend.\"\n                )\n            if decode_backend == \"trtllm_mha\" and not (\n                is_sm90_supported() or is_sm100_supported() or is_sm120_supported()\n            ):\n                raise ValueError(\n                    \"TRTLLM MHA backend for decode is only supported on Hopper (SM90), Blackwell (SM100) and (SM120) GPUs. Please use a different decode backend.\"\n                )\n\n        run_post_process_pass(self, _attention_backend_fa3_fp8_fallback)\n\n        run_post_process_pass(self, _fa4_page_constraint)\n\n        # AMD platforms backends\n        if resolved_view(self).attention_backend == \"aiter\":\n            if model_config.context_len > 8192:\n                self.mem_fraction_static *= 0.85\n\n        # Other platforms backends\n        run_post_process_pass(self, _attention_backend_platform_fallbacks)\n\n        prefill_backend, decode_backend = self._resolved_attention_backends()\n        if self.use_mla_backend() and prefill_backend == \"intel_xpu\":\n            raise ValueError(\n                \"intel_xpu backend is only supported on decode for MLA models, please set --decode-attention-backend to intel_xpu and do not set --attention-backend or --prefill-attention-backend to intel_xpu for prefill instead use triton.\"\n            )\n\n        run_post_process_pass(self, _intel_xpu_page_constraint)\n\n        #             layer.w2_weight,\n                topk_weights,\n                topk_ids,\n                False,  # inplace # See [Note] inplace should be False in fused_experts.\n moe_runner_config,\n            )\n            return StandardCombineInput(hidden_states=output)\n\n    def get_triton_quant_info(self, layer: torch.nn.Module) -> TritonMoeQuantInfo:\n        return Install an aiter build that \"\n                \"supports fused_moe no_combine output.\"\n            )\n\n        if runner_input.hidden_states.shape[0] == 0:\n            if self.config.no_combine:\n                topk = runner_input.topk_ids.shape[-1]\n                hidden_size = runner_input.hidden_states.shape[-1]\n                return AiterRunnerOutput(\n                    hidden_states=runner_input.hidden_states.new_empty(\n                        (0, topk, hidden_size)\n                    )\n                )\n            return AiterRunnerOutput(hidden_states=runner_input.hidden_states)\n\n        from aiter.fused_moe import fused_moe\n\n        from sglang.srt.environ import envs\n\n        a1_scale = (\n            runner_input.a1_scale\n            if runner_input.a1_scale is not None\n            else quant_info.a13_scale\n        )\n\n        extra: dict = {}\n        if quant_info.fused_moe_kwargs:\n            extra.update(quant_info.fused_moe_kwargs)\n        if runner_input.num_local_tokens is not None:\n            extra[\"num_local_tokens\"] = runner_input.num_local_tokens\n        if runner_input.output_dtype is not None:\n            extra[\"dtype\"] = runner_input.output_dtype\n        if quant_info.swiglu_limit > 0:\n            # GateMode is only needed for the gpt-oss MXFP4 swiglu_limit path.\n            # Import lazily so models that don't use it (e.g. DeepSeek-V3 fp8,\n            # swiglu_limit==0) still run on aiter builds where this module\n            # lives elsewhere / is absent.\n            from aiter.ops.flydsl.moe_common import GateMode\n\n            # Default (INTERLEAVE) preserves the pre-fix behavior for paths\n            # that prepare weights in the gate/up-interleaved layout. Set\n            # `SGLANG_USE_AITER_MOE_GU_ITLV=0` to switch to SEPARATED, which\n            # matches the layout produced by `Mxfp4MoEMethod` (gpt-oss\n            # MXFP4) and the gptoss_fp4 tuned FlyDSL kernels.\n            extra[\"gate_mode\"] = (\n                GateMode.INTERLEAVE.value\n                if envs.SGLANG_USE_AITER_MOE_GU_ITLV.get()\n                else GateMode.SEPARATED.value\n            )\n            extra[\"swiglu_limit\"] = quant_info.swiglu_limit\n        if self.config.no_combine:\n            extra[\"no_combine\"] = True\n\n        output =        topk_ids=runner_input.topk_ids,\n            quant_type=_aiter_quant_type(runner_input.quant_type),\n            activation=_aiter_activation(self.config.activation),\n            w1_scale=quant_info.w13_scale,\n            w2_scale=quant_info.w2_scale,\n            a1_scale=a1_scale,\n            a2_scale=quant_info.a2_scale,\n            bias1=quant_info.b13,\n            bias2=quant_info.b2,\n            expert_mask=quant_info.expert_mask,\n            doweight_stage1=quant_info.doweight_stage1,\n            hidden_pad=quant_info.hidden_pad,\n            intermediate_pad=quant_info.intermediate_pad,\n            **extra,\n        )\n        return AiterRunnerOutput(hidden_states=output)\n\n    @property\n    def runner_backend(self) -> MoeRunnerBackend:\n        return MoeRunnerBackend.AITER\n\n\n# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n# Pre-permute: dispatch_output -> AiterRunnerInput\n# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n\n\n@register_pre_permute(\"standard\", \"aiter\")\ndef pre_permute_standard_to_aiter(\n    dispatch_output: StandardDispatchOutput,\n    quant_info: AiterMoeQuantInfo,\n    runner_config: MoeRunnerConfig,\n    running_state: dict,\n) -> AiterRunnerInput:\n    hidden_states = dispatch_output.hidden_states\n    topk_weights, topk_ids, _ = dispatch_output.topk_output\n    topk_weights = topk_weights.to(torch.float32)\n\n    if runner_config.apply_router_weight_on_input and not quant_info.doweight_stage1:\n        # Pre-scale at the Python level for kernels that don't honor doweight_stage1.\n        assert (\n            topk_weights.dim() == 2 and topk_weights.shape[-1] == 1\n        ), \"apply_router_weight_on_input requires topk=1\"\n        hidden_states = hidden_states * topk_weights.to(hidden_states.dtype)\n        topk_weights = torch.ones_like(topk_weights)\n\n    return AiterRunnerInput(\n        hidden_states=hidden_states,\n        topk_ids=topk_ids.to(torch.int32),\n        topk_weights=topk_weights,\n        quant_type=quant_info.quant_type,\n    )\n\n\ndef _is_mori_dispatch_output(dispatch_output: Any) -> bool:\n    # MoriEP{Normal,LL}DispatchOutput carry the post-mori-permute origin_topk_*\n    # tensors that the standard DeepEP outputs lack.\n    return hasattr(dispatch_output, \"origin_topk_ids\")\n\n\ndef _resolve_mori_quant_type(\n    dispatch_a1_dtype: torch.dtype,\n    dispatch_scale: Optional[torch.Tensor],\n    weight_quant: AiterQuantType,\n) -> AiterQuantType:\n    \"\"\"Pick the activation quant_type for AITER when the dispatch path may have\n    pre-quantized hidden_states. Mirrors the original MoriEPMoE.run_moe_core\n    decision tree.\"\"\"\n    is_fp8_quant = weight_quant in (\n        AiterQuantType.PER_128X128,\n        AiterQuantType.PER_TOKEN,\n    )\n    is_w4a4 = weight_quant == AiterQuantType.PER_1X32\n    is_fp4_dispatch = dispatch_a1_dtype == torch.float4_e2m1fn_x2\n    has_dispatch_scale = dispatch_scale is not None\n\n    if is_w4a4:\n        # W4A4 weights always run as per_1x32; FP8 dispatch is upscaled to BF16\n        # before this point so dispatch_scale won't conflict.\n        return AiterQuantType.PER_1X32\n    if is_fp8_quant:\n        return weight_quant\n    # BF16 weights: lift to the dispatch-side quant type when scales are provided.\n    if has_dispatch_scale and is_fp4_dispatch:\n        return AiterQuantType.PER_1X32\n    if has_dispatch_scale and not is_fp4_dispatch:\n        return AiterQuantType.PER_128X128\n    return AiterQuantType.NONE\n\n\ndef _pre_permute_deepep_to_aiter(\n    dispatch_output: Union[\n        DeepEPNormalDispatchOutput,\n        DeepEPLLDispatchOutput,\n        MoriEPNormalDispatchOutput,\n        MoriEPLLDispatchOutput,\n    ],\n    quant_info: AiterMoeQuantInfo,\n    runner_config: MoeRunnerConfig,\n    running_state: dict,\n) -> AiterRunnerInput:\n    is_mori = _is_mori_dispatch_output(dispatch_output)\n\n    hidden_states = dispatch_output.hidden_states\n    topk_ids = dispatch_output.topk_ids.to(torch.int32)\n    topk_weights = dispatch_output.topk_weights.to(torch.float32)\n    a1_scale: Optional[torch.Tensor] = None\n    num_local_tokens: Optional[torch.Tensor] = None\n    output_dtype: Optional[torch.dtype] = None\n    quant_type = quant_info.quant_type\n\n    if is_mori:\n        from sglang.srt.layers.moe.rocm_moe_utils import upscale, upscale_mxfp4\n\n        a1_scale = dispatch_output.hidden_states_scale\n        num_local_tokens = dispatch_output.num_recv_tokens_per_expert\n        output_dtype = dispatch_output.out_dtype\n\n        # Truncate dispatch tensors to the configured cap; mori combine only\n        # reads [0, totalRecvTokenNum), so the truncated result needs no\n        # padding back.\n        mori_max = get_int_env_var(\"SGLANG_MORI_MOE_MAX_INPUT_TOKENS\", 0)\n        if mori_max > 0:\n            hidden_states = hidden_states[:mori_max]\n            if a1_scale is not None:\n                a1_scale = a1_scale[:mori_max]\n            topk_ids = topk_ids[:mori_max]\n            topk_weights = topk_weights[:mori_max]\n\n        # Upscale dispatched activations when there is no AITER kernel for the\n        # weight/activation dtype pair.\n        weight_quant = quant_info.quant_type\n        is_fp8_quant = weight_quant in (\n            AiterQuantType.PER_128X128,\n            AiterQuantType.PER_TOKEN,\n        )\n        is_w4a4 = weight_quant == AiterQuantType.PER_1X32\n        is_fp4_dispatch = hidden_states.dtype == torch.float4_e2m1fn_x2\n\n        # AITER fused_moe Clamped-SwiGLU is dispatched with\n        # gate_mode=INTERLEAVE, for which AITER picks a bf16/fp8 `q_dtype_a`\n        # Refer to https://github.com/ROCm/aiter/blob/a2617c366dc7271a1662ecda2023d19f6ccefcec/aiter/fused_moe.py#L406-L412\n        swiglu_interleave = quant_info.swiglu_limit > 0 and get_bool_env_var(\n            \"SGLANG_USE_AITER_MOE_GU_ITLV\", \"true\"\n        )\n\n        if is_w4a4 and a1_scale is not None and not is_fp4_dispatch:\n            # W4A4 weights with FP8 dispatch: dequant FP8->BF16 first; the\n            # FP4 per_1x32 path needs BF16 input.\n            hidden_states = upscale(\n                hidden_states, a1_scale, num_local_tokens, output_dtype\n            )\n            a1_scale = None\n        elif is_w4a4 and is_fp4_dispatch and a1_scale is not None and swiglu_interleave:\n            # W4A4 weights + FP4 dispatch on the clamped-SwiGLU/INTERLEAVE\n            # path: AITER expects a bf16/fp8 activation here, not fp4x2.\n            # Dequant FP4->BF16 and let fused_moe re-quantize internally.\n            hidden_states = upscale_mxfp4(\n                hidden_states, a1_scale, num_local_tokens, output_dtype\n            )\n            a1_scale = None\n        elif is_fp8_quant and is_fp4_dispatch and a1_scale is not None:\n            # FP8 weights + FP4 dispatch: no kernel for the fp4x2/fp8 pair;\n            # dequant FP4->BF16 and let fused_moe re-quantize to FP8.\n            hidden_states = upscale_mxfp4(\n                hidden_states, a1_scale, num_local_tokens, output_dtype\n            )\n            a1_scale = None\n\n        quant_type = _resolve_mori_quant_type(\n            hidden_states.dtype, a1_scale, weight_quant\n        )\n\n        running_state[\"aiter_combine_topk_ids\"] = dispatch_output.origin_topk_ids\n        running_state[\"aiter_combine_topk_weights\"] = (\n            dispatch_output.origin_topk_weights\n        )\n    else:\n        # DeepEP marks invalid topk slots with idx == -1; AITER cannot accept\n        # negative ids, so reroute them to the sink slot at index\n        # num_local_experts (masked off by quant_info.expert_mask which has\n        # shape (num_local_experts + 1,)).\n        topk_ids = torch.where(\n            topk_ids == -1,\n            torch.full_like(topk_ids, runner_config.num_local_experts),\n            topk_ids,\n        )\n        running_state[\"aiter_combine_topk_ids\"] = dispatch_output.topk_ids\n        running_state[\"aiter_combine_topk_weights\"] = dispatch_output.topk_weights\n\n    running_state[\"aiter_combine_is_mori\"] = is_mori\n\n    return AiterRunnerInput(\n        hidden_states=hidden_states,\n        topk_ids=topk_ids,\n        topk_weights=topk_weights,\n        quant_type=quant_type,\n        a1_scale=a1_scale,\n        num_local_tokens=num_local_tokens,\n        output_dtype=output_dtype,\n    )\n\n\nregister_pre_permute(\"deepep_normal\", \"aiter\")(_pre_permute_deepep_to_aiter)\nregister_pre_permute(\"deepep_ll\", \"aiter\")(_pre_permute_deepep_to_aiter)\n\n\n# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n# Post-permute: AiterRunnerOutput -> CombineInput\n# Then implement changes.\n\nBefore changes, we need understand if aiter attention is possible. We can try a quick manual test: set attention backend to aiter by modifying override? We can also test via environment? For Gemma4, attention_backend defaults to triton due override. But we can change override to aiter. Let's test that.\n\nBut maybe aiter attention backend has issue with model's RoPE proportional for full_attention. The rope variant \"proportional\" with partial_rotary_factor 0.25: Q/K head_dim 256 but rotary applies to first 64 dims? In Gemma4 code, get_rope is called with partial_rotary_factor from config, base etc. Aiter backend uses its own RoPE? Actually MHA backends use pre-applied rotary embedding (q/k have rope applied before attention). So attention backend doesn't care about rope type; the model applies rotary. Aiter backend just computes attention on q,k,v. So no issue.\n\nAiter backend may not support `logit_cap`? Gemma4 has no logit_cap. Fine.\n\nSliding window: aiter backend uses `layer.sliding_window_size` from RadixAttention. It supports. For full layers, no sliding window. For SWA layers, window size 1023. The KV cache uses SWAKVPool with vectorized 5D maybe. Aiter uses `use_sliding_window_kv_pool` and handles.\n\nOne risk: aiter backend's `forward_extend` (prefill) for sliding window uses `extend_attention_fwd` (triton) unless use_triton_unified_attention. With SWAKVPool, `use_triton_unified_attention = True` (line 249). That path uses `unified_attention` from aiter for decode but for prefill maybe uses triton extend_attention_fwd. The prefill for sliding window may still be triton. But full attention prefill could use aiter `mha_batch_prefill_func` (CK) which is faster.\n\nAlternatively, the bottleneck in TTFT for long input may be full attention layers (long context) or all layers. Sliding window layers have small KV context length 1024, cheap. Full layers have long context 8192; prefill compute O(n^2) there. Aiter full prefill might improve.\n\nLet's change Gemma4 override to default to aiter on HIP. In `arg_groups/overrides.py`, `_gemma4_overrides` currently sets default_attention_backend = \"trtllm_mha\" if is_sm100 else \"triton\". On HIP, we want \"aiter\". We can change to:\n```\nif is_hip():\n    default_attention_backend = \"aiter\"\nelif is_sm100_supported():\n    default_attention_backend = \"trtllm_mha\"\nelse:\n    default_attention_backend = \"triton\"\n```\nBut we must be sure aiter is available and compatible. On HIP, `is_aiter` is a registered backend. If aiter import fails, server will crash. But aiter is installed.\n\nBut maybe Gemma4 on aiter has known accuracy issue? We can test with accuracy (GSM8K) after. If accuracy passes, fine.\n\nLet's also consider enabling flash attention via xformers? No.\n\nAnother optimization: The `triton_attention_num_kv_splits` default is 16 for HIP; maybe aiter uses its own splits. Not relevant if switching to aiter.\n\nAnother potential: The model uses `Gemma4MLP` which is Gemma3MLP. We can fuse activation and gate/up using aiter? Already linear uses aiter tgemm maybe but activation is separate? Gemma3MLP likely uses `GemmaRMSNorm` then `gate_up_proj` then `down_proj`, activation `GELU`. Not a SwiGLU. Could fuse gate_proj+up_proj+activation? Not straightforward with GELU. Probably fine.\n\nMoE not using aiter due alignment; maybe we can pad intermediate size to 128 by modifying `moe_intermediate_size` in config? But model weights have size 704. If we pad weights to 768 with zeros for extra 64, the MoE output unchanged (extra dims zero). But aiter CK fused MoE may require exact padded shape. We can adjust config to 768 and pad weights during loading. That would enable aiter fused MoE and preserve accuracy (if extra dims zero and no bias). However, is it safe? Need see if `intermediate_size` is used elsewhere (e.g., loading weights). We could add a config hook to round up `moe_intermediate_size` to 128 and pad weights. But the verifier uses model path; config read from model. We can modify Gemma4MoE to compute `intermediate_size_padded` and pad weights after loading? But aiter CK fused MoE uses config's `intermediate_size`. In `Gemma4MoE.__init__`, it passes `intermediate_size=config.moe_intermediate_size` to `FusedMoE`. We can override `intermediate_size` to padded value there, and in load_weights pad experts. But then the checkpoint has 704; we'd need to remap names. Complex.\n\nMaybe simpler: use `triton_kernels` MoE backend if available and supports 704. We can default moe_runner_backend to triton_kernel for Gemma4 on HIP. But must test accuracy.\n\nLet's first test attention backend change. We'll modify overrides, kill server, relaunch, run benchmark.\n\nBefore modifying, maybe we should explore aiter attention quickly by setting env `SGLANG_USE_AITER_UNIFIED_ATTN=1`? That only affects aiter backend's decode unified attention, not prefill. Not needed.\n\nLet's modify overrides.","time":{"start":1784477201362,"end":1784477241345}}}

if expert_id is None, then\n        # all the experts are loaded at the same time\n        if (\n            not expert_id\n            and self.quant_config is not None\n            and self.quant_config.get_name() == \"mxfp4\"\n            and self.quant_config.is_static_cfg()\n        ):\n            if \"bias\" in weight_name:\n                dim1 = loaded_weight.shape[1]\n                param.data[:, :dim1].copy_(loaded_weight)\n            else:\n                dim1 = loaded_weight.shape[1]\n                dim2 = loaded_weight.shape[2]\n                param.data[:, :dim1, :dim2].copy_(loaded_weight)\n            return\n\n        global_expert_location_metadata = get_global_expert_location_metadata()\n        if global_expert_location_metadata is None:\n            if not getattr(param, \"_sglang_require_global_experts\", False):\n                expert_id = self._map_global_expert_id_to_local_expert_id(expert_id)\n                if expert_id == -1:\n                    return\n\n            self._weight_loader_impl(\n                param=param,\n                loaded_weight=loaded_weight,\n                weight_name=weight_name,\n                shard_id=shard_id,\n                expert_id=expert_id,\n            )\n            return\n\n        require_global_experts = getattr(param, \"_sglang_require_global_experts\", False)\n        shared_expert_id = (\n            expert_id - global_expert_location_metadata.num_logical_experts\n            if self._has_fused_shared and expert_id is not None\n            else -1\n        )\n        if 0 <= shared_expert_id < self.num_fused_shared_experts:\n            # Checkpoint shared experts start after logical routed experts, while\n            # local fused MoE weights store them after physical routed experts.\n            if require_global_experts and uses_per_rank_fused_shared_slots():\n                physical_expert_ids = [\n                    rank * self.num_local_experts\n                    + self._num_local_routed\n                    + shared_expert_id\n                    for rank in range(self.moe_ep_size)\n                ]\n            else:\n                physical_expert_ids = [self._num_global_routed + shared_expert_id]\n        else:\n            physical_expert_ids = (\n                global_expert_location_metadata.logical_to_all_physical(\n                    self.layer_id, expert_id, require_global_experts\n                )\n            )\n\n        for physical_expert_id in physical_expert_ids:\n            self._weight_loader_physical(\n                param=param,\n                loaded_weight=loaded_weight,\n                weight_name=weight_name,\n                shard_id=shard_id,\n                expert_id=physical_expert_id,\n            )\n\n    def _weight_loader_physical(\n        self,\n        param: torch.nn.Parameter,\n        loaded_weight: torch.Tensor,\n        weight_name: str,\n        shard_id: str,\n        expert_id: int,\n    ) -> None:\n        # WARN: This makes the `expert_id` mean \"local\" and \"global\" in different cases\n        if not getattr(param, \"_sglang_require_global_experts\", False):\n            expert_id = self._map_global_expert_id_to_local_expert_id(expert_id)\n            if expert_id < 0 or expert_id >= self.num_local_experts:\n                return\n\n        if isinstance(\n            self.quant_method,\n            KTEPWrapperMethod,\n        ):\n            if self.quant_method.num_gpu_experts != -1:\n                if expert_id >= self.quant_method.num_gpu_experts:\n                    return\n\n        self._weight_loader_impl(\n            param=param,\n            loaded_weight=loaded_weight,\n            weight_name=weight_name,\n            shard_id=shard_id,\n            expert_id=expert_id,\n        )\n\n    def _load_gguf_weight(\n        self,\n        param: torch.nn.Parameter,\n        loaded_weight: torch.Tensor,\n        shard_id: str,\n        expert_id: int,\n        tp_rank: int,\n    ) -> bool:\n        \"\"\"Handle GGUF weight loading.\n\n        Args:\n            param: The parameter to load the weight into.\n            loaded_weight: The weight tensor to load.\n            shard_id: The shard ID (w1, w2, or w3).\n            expert_id: The expert ID.\n            tp_rank: The tensor parallel rank.\n\n        Returns:\n            True if the weight was handled as a GGUF weight, False otherwise.\n        \"\"\"\n        is_gguf_weight = getattr(param, \"is_gguf_weight\", False)\n        is_gguf_weight_type = getattr(param, \"is_gguf_weight_type\", False)\n\n        if is_gguf_weight_type:\n            # Store weight type for this expert\n            param.weight_type = loaded_weight.item()\n            return True\n\n        if is_gguf_weight:\n            output_dim = getattr(param, \"output_dim\", None)\n            if self.moe_tp_size > 1:\n                if shard_id in [\"w1\", \"w3\", \"w2\"] and output_dim == 0:\n                    shard_size = loaded_weight.size(0) // self.moe_tp_size\n                    start_idx = tp_rank * shard_size\n                    loaded_weight = loaded_weight.narrow(\n                        0, start_idx, shard_size\n                    ).clone()\n\n            #
